races.race_name: Dragon Symbol Standing At Whitsbury Manor British EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Galipi
runners.spotlight: Shaped with promise in finishing 2l third of five at Ffos Las (7.4f, good to soft) where he was the sole newcomer in the field and well-backed favourite; the form pick and evidently thought capable of better to boot.
runners.comment: Shaped with promise at Ffos Las where he was a well-backed favourite; the form pick

runners.name: See The Mountain
runners.spotlight: 70,000gns yearling; by Kameko; dam 1m2f scorer (RPR 81), half-sister to Irish Derby winner Latrobe; represents major yard; one of the likelier types among the newcomers.
runners.comment: 70,000gns yearling; by Kameko; major yard; one of the likelier types among the newcomers

runners.name: Breacher
runners.spotlight: Newcomer by Cracksman out of 11.6f/1m4f winner (RPR 88) who hitherto hasn't made much of a splash as a broodmare; others preferred.
runners.comment: Out of a middle-distance winner who hitherto hasn't made much of a splash as a broodmare

runners.name: Rafe's Da Man
runners.spotlight: Achieved a meagre RPR in finishing 17l fourth of six at Lingfield (7f, good) but seemed to need the run; bigger effort may be on the way.
runners.comment: Achieved a meagre RPR at Lingfield but seemed to need the run

runners.name: Spruce
runners.spotlight: Advertise half-brother to 5f scorer Dothan (RPR 81); dam maiden (63), closely related to 5f Group 3 winner Dutch Masterpiece; market informative.
runners.comment: Advertise half-brother to 5f scorer; market informative

runners.name: Jiro
runners.spotlight: Dropped away and trailed home last of six in 6f event at Newmarket, having started the rank outsider; again best watched.
runners.comment: Dropped away and trailed home last of six in 6f event at Newmarket

runners.name: Power Effort
runners.spotlight: Debutant who is the son of winners for his connections (sire 5f-7f, dam 1m2f); the betting may offer further guidance.
runners.comment: Debutant who is the son of winners for his connections

runners.name: Squadron
runners.spotlight: Juddmonte colt, by Frankel out of 7f-9.5f winner (including 2yo/US turf Grade 1; RPR 109), from family of Dancing Brave; respected debutant, especially if the market speaks favourably.
runners.comment: Colt by Frankel out of US Grade 1 winner, from family of Dancing Brave; respected newcomer

races.race_name: Whitsbury Manor Stud Supporting Inspire Foundation Handicap

runners.name: Silver Wraith
runners.spotlight: Form dipped last time but that was out of character; consistent sort otherwise and ran respectably, when bidding for a Bath hat-trick, the time before; may rebound.
runners.comment: Form dipped last time but that was out of character; consistent sort otherwise

runners.name: Connie's Rose
runners.spotlight: Seven-time winner at Chepstow and finished a solid second, splitting subsequent winners, there last time; has form over this C&D and warrants respect up just 1lb.
runners.comment: Solid second, splitting subsequent winners, at Chepstow last time; has form over C&D

runners.name: Cayman Tai
runners.spotlight: Below par at Sandown this month but a return to his May form, namely an AW success flanked by second-place finishes on turf, would put him in the picture.
runners.comment: Return to his May form (212) would put him in the picture

runners.name: The Thames Boatman
runners.spotlight: Recent form includes a turf success but he's only 1-19 in this sphere and still has a much better strike-rate on AW; below par at Brighton last time.
runners.comment: Still has a much better strike-rate on AW than on turf; below par last time

runners.name: Fresh Fade
runners.spotlight: 6f winner in Ireland last term; holds weak claims on his two efforts for new stable; opposed unless the betting suggests a resurgence is on the cards.
runners.comment: Holds weak claims on his two efforts for new stable

runners.name: King Of Stars
runners.spotlight: Formerly useful; hasn't won since 2021 and ended last season with a pair of duck eggs; best watched on reappearance, unless the market suggests otherwise.
runners.comment: Hasn't won since 2021 and ended last season with two duck eggs; reappearance

races.race_name: Juddmonte EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C And D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Harry Knows
runners.spotlight: Threatening to win a race, having finished a head second in two of his three starts, gelded prior to getting nabbed in the final strides by Flying Pirate over C&D latest; gets a 4lb pull with that opponent; leading player at the weights.
runners.comment: Better off with Flying Pirate on C&D running last time and holds leading claims
runners.owner: Thames Boys, J Henwood & J Clay

runners.name: Metamouse
runners.spotlight: Twice-raced colt who is open to further improvement; scored comfortably at Chester (6f, good to soft) to build on his Chepstow effort; one of the main form contenders.
runners.comment: Scored comfortably at Chester last time and looks open to further improvement

runners.name: Flying Pirate
runners.spotlight: Overcame greenness to collar Harry Knows in the final strides in C&D contest on soft ground; 4lb worse off with that rival and concedes weight all round but may be up to the task, with progress very plausible.
runners.comment: Overcame greenness to collar Harry Knows in C&D contest; progress looks plausible

runners.name: Poppy Foxy
runners.spotlight: Avoids a penalty for seller win at Chester (7f, good to soft) but still needs further improvement to follow up in this stronger field and possibly isn't crying out for the drop back in distance.
runners.comment: Winner of Chester seller last time; needs further improvement to follow up

runners.name: Treasurer
runners.spotlight: Has shown promise over 6f in both starts, catching the eye at Nottingham then seeming to run well on the figures (albeit was never better than midfield) in the Coventry at Royal Ascot; interesting back down in class.
runners.comment: Caught the eye at Nottingham then seemed to run well in the Coventry; interesting

runners.name: Keep Grating
runners.spotlight: 35,000euros yearling; Space Traveller half-brother to four winners, notably Dazzling Des (5f-6f including 2yo/Italy; RPR 85); dam 5f 2yo scorer (91); one of two newcomers in the field; market instructive.
runners.comment: 35,000euros yearling; by Space Traveller; one of two newcomers in the field

runners.name: Calendar Boy
runners.spotlight: 6,500euros foal, 19,000gns yearling; by Gregorian and first foal of unraced half-sister to 7f 2yo Listed winner Aqlaam Vision; this debutant is a stablemate of C&D scorer Flying Pirate; market informative.
runners.comment: 19,000gns yearling; by Gregorian; stablemate of Flying Pirate; market helpful

runners.name: Ten Year Stretch
runners.spotlight: Looked green on his way to finishing seventh of 11 in C&D event; recorded a modest RPR so needs to take a big step forward.
runners.comment: Looked green in C&D event; needs to improve a lot on bare form

runners.name: Martha Brown
runners.spotlight: Finished third of five at Bath (5.7f) last time, improving on debut effort but registering another meagre RPR; opposed.
runners.comment: Registered another meagre RPR last time

runners.name: Ten Of Clubs
runners.spotlight: Only eighth of nine in 6f AW event at Wolverhampton, having been one of the outsiders; perhaps one for nurseries later on.
runners.comment: Only eighth of nine in AW event

runners.name: Herbert Hall
runners.spotlight: May be sharper with Newbury outing (6.5f) under his belt but needs a big step forward, having ended up last of nine in that race.
runners.comment: Ended up last of nine at Newbury and needs a big step forward

races.race_name: Havana Grey Standing At Whitsbury Manor Handicap

runners.name: Addison Grey
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 4yo whose sound form this term includes a respectable effort behind the subsequent Wokingham winner; one of his three wins was gained over C&D; solid chance.
runners.comment: Sound form this term includes a respectable effort behind the subsequent Wokingham winner

runners.name: Fleetwater
runners.spotlight: Has performed creditably a few times this term but her form surprisingly dipped at Windsor last time, has gained both wins at that venue; others preferred.
runners.comment: Form surprisingly dipped at Windsor last time; has gained both wins there
runners.owner: Ms H N Pinniger And Partner

runners.name: Another Abbot
runners.spotlight: Narrowly beat a subsequent scorer (pair clear) at Yarmouth last time, taking his record over 6f to 3-6; may be capable of further progress kept to this distance; respected.
runners.comment: Beat a subsequent scorer (pair clear) last time, taking 6f record to 3-6; respected

runners.name: Queue Dos
runners.spotlight: Campaigned mostly in lower grades and has done her winning on Wolverhampton AW; doesn't look the answer in this scenario.
runners.comment: Has done her winning on Wolverhampton AW; doesn't look the answer

runners.name: Sudden Flight
runners.spotlight: Still seeking first win on turf but has posted two encouraging efforts this term, going close in first-time cheekpieces at Goodwood latest; possibilities in the retained headgear.
runners.comment: Two encouraging efforts this term, going close in first-time cheekpieces latest

runners.name: Jel Pepper
runners.spotlight: Registered his success in a 2yo sales race over 7f; seemed unsuited by the drop back to 6f last time (handicap debut) but this stiffer track may help; 2lb lower.
runners.comment: Seemed unsuited by the drop back to 6f last time but this stiffer track may help

runners.name: Egoli
runners.spotlight: Highly tried last term after novice success; never figured in Windsor handicap on reappearance but may fare better with the outing under his belt; check the betting.
runners.comment: May fare better with Windsor reappearance under his belt; check the betting

races.race_name: Madar Corporation EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Storming Point
runners.spotlight: Thrice-raced colt who is proving consistent; good second in both turf attempts, latest at Windsor (1m, good to soft) behind a useful sort; leading form contender upped further in distance.
runners.comment: Thrice-raced colt who is proving consistent; leading player upped further in trip

runners.name: Zachary Hickes
runners.spotlight: Eye-catching fourth at Kempton (AW) in late 2024, then was absent until sixth of 14 at Windsor (1m2f) this month; remains open to improvement, with the reappearance effort under his belt.
runners.comment: Remains open to improvement, with Windsor reappearance under his belt

runners.name: Aleatrix
runners.spotlight: Study Of Man half-sister to four winners, notably Ebury (1m; RPR 100); dam 1m4f scorer (82), closely related to Derby runner-up Dragon Dancer; represents esteemed connections; interesting newcomer.
runners.comment: Study Of Man half-sister to four winners; represents esteemed connections; interesting

runners.name: Paniolo Creek
runners.spotlight: 5yo by Eastern Anthem; first foal; dam unplaced 1m/1m2f in France, half-sister to smart 2m/2m3f hurdle winner Sempre Medici; probably best watched on belated debut, unless the market suggests otherwise.
runners.comment: 5yo who is probably best watched on belated debut

runners.name: Al Qatem
runners.spotlight: Failed to live up to expectations (4-5 favourite, finished only eighth of nine) in 8.5f AW maiden at Wolverhampton but is evidently thought capable of much better; big step forward is not out of the question.
runners.comment: Disappointing favourite in AW maiden but is evidently thought capable of better

runners.name: Virtue Diligence
runners.spotlight: Twice-raced gelding who gives the firm impression that handicaps will be more suitable shortly.
runners.comment: Should find handicaps more suitable shortly

runners.name: Sevenkingsmustdie
runners.spotlight: Holds weak claims on the form he has shown in two bumpers and a Flat contest.
runners.comment: Holds weak claims on form

races.race_name: Showcasing Standing At Whitsbury Manor Bibury Cup Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Pearl River
runners.spotlight: Has a record of 2-3 in handicaps, the wins over 1m2f at Nottingham (good to soft) as a 2yo and York (good) in latest outing; likely to be suited by this new trip and is the proven handicapper in the field; possibly hasn't reached his ceiling yet.
runners.comment: The proven handicapper in the field and possibly hasn't reached his ceiling yet

runners.name: Mythical Valentine
runners.spotlight: Has shown steady progress in five maiden races, as form figures of 321 this season reflect; form of half-length win at Windsor (11.5f, good to firm) was franked when the runner-up went one better next time; goes handicapping with a solid chance.
runners.comment: Steady progress in maidens; form of Windsor win has been franked; solid chance

runners.name: Gone By
runners.spotlight: Thrice-raced filly and Irish Oaks entry whose form includes a promising effort behind a useful stablemate on debut; very easily justified favouritism at Ripon (1m3f, good) most recently; likely to build on that win and show further improvement; respected.
runners.comment: Irish Oaks entry who should build on Ripon success and improve further; respected

races.race_name: Molson Coors Handicap

runners.name: Hot And Cold
runners.spotlight: Prescott filly who has been absent since making all in Newcastle novice event (1m, AW) on final 2yo start, beating a useful rival who currently has a mark of 102; the type to progress further and do well in handicaps; very interesting off an opening mark of 75, especially if the market speaks positively.
runners.comment: Prescott filly who beat a useful sort on final 2yo start; interesting on reappearance

runners.name: Lyra Lea
runners.spotlight: Has shown most of her form over 7f, including AW maiden success as a 2yo and good second at Chepstow (soft) most recently; may be effective back up in trip, provided she settles (sometimes takes a good hold).
runners.comment: Has shown most of her form over 7f, good second at Chepstow most recently

runners.name: Station Bar
runners.spotlight: Beat only two rivals at Bath (1m, firm) to open his account at the ninth attempt; however, may build on that success and do better still, in keeping with his pedigree (from a family that has served his connections well); shortlisted.
runners.comment: Pedigree gives optimism that he can build on Bath win and do better still; shortlisted

runners.name: Legacy Rock
runners.spotlight: Maiden prior to winning twice at Windsor (good to firm) and running creditably at Nottingham (good) since upped to 1m; creeping up the weights but may remain competitive kept to this trip.
runners.comment: Maiden prior to posting form figures of 112 since upped to 1m; may remain competitive

runners.name: John Harrison
runners.spotlight: Absent and gelded since running well at Lingfield (1m, AW; handicap debut) in January; another step forward is required but market support should be heeded.
runners.comment: Absent and gelded since running well in January; heed the market signals

runners.name: Dark Sovereign
runners.spotlight: Four-race maiden who has consistent 7f form, fourth in Sandown handicap most recently; possibilities, provided this extra furlong proves a source of progress.
runners.comment: Has consistent 7f form; possibilities, provided this extra furlong prompts progress

races.race_name: Dunbrody Beginners Chase

runners.name: Scalpnagoon
runners.spotlight: Jack Kennedy's first ride back after a layoff; dual winning hurdler and fair level of form over fences, although still not the most fluent at times; remote third behind two useful sorts in 2m novice at Roscommon last time; looks beatable.
runners.comment: Fair level of form over fences, although still not the most fluent; Kennedy returns on him

runners.name: William Tell
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to Ben Pauling's smart 3m chaser Henry's Friend; two wins over hurdles last year at up to 2m6f; made mistakes in his point-to-points before that and this trip will be too sharp for him on chase debut.
runners.comment: Half-brother to smart 3m chaser Henry's Friend; dual hurdles winner but 2m looks too short

runners.name: Impero
runners.spotlight: Smart five-time winner over hurdles and ran well in two useful handicaps in May; 3-1, jumped well out in front before tiring on chase debut over 2m4f at Kilbeggan; could be hard to beat over this trip.
runners.comment: Jumped well out in front before tiring on chase debut over 2m4f at Kilbeggan

runners.name: Nadia's Boy
runners.spotlight: Won a point and bumper and made all to beat the useful Frankie John on hurdling debut at the Listowel festival; not seen after until meeting defeat for the first time at the Punchestown festival, too keen after his absence and dropping right out; chase debut.
runners.comment: Point, bumper and maiden hurdle winner where he made all to beat the useful Frankie John

runners.name: Soir De Garde
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to top-class State Man made a winning hurdles debut at Tramore last year; ran a decent race behind stablemate El Fabiolo at Leopardstown in March; fading in third when falling at the last at Cork and poor jumping took its toll when pulled up at Punchestown; goes chasing now; has twice been scratched on good-to-yielding ground, which is a concern.
runners.comment: Half-brother to State Man won over hurdles at Tramore last year; jumping issues lately

runners.name: Wendrock
runners.spotlight: Useful juvenile hurdler the season before last; best run last term when fifth off 130 in 2m4f handicap at Leopardstown over Christmas; tailed off over 3m1f on chase debut, but he's definitely not a two-miler either; headgear.
runners.comment: Tailed off over 3m1f on chase debut, but he's definitely not a two-miler either; headgear

races.race_name: Tintern Trails Handicap Chase

runners.name: Femme Magnifique
runners.spotlight: Bumper and hurdles winner for Willie Mullins in 2023-24; didn't add to her tally for Paul Kiely last year but came back to form on debut for this yard, winning a 2m Tramore handicap chase, beating Presenting Lad; up 6lb but can be a player with a repeat.
runners.comment: Won a 2m Tramore handicap chase last time; up 6lb but can be a player with a repeat

runners.name: Solsbury Hill
runners.spotlight: Won a Leopardstown maiden hurdle and Roscommon beginners' chase last year; below that level in three chase starts since, so has to be at his best after an absence; has run well over hurdles here; tongue-tie added.
runners.comment: Below par in last three chase starts so has to be at his best after an absence; tongue-tie

runners.name: Trasna Na Pairce
runners.spotlight: Won twice over hurdles at Clonmel last year up to 2m6f; sent chasing after and some decent efforts in defeat including over 2m4f at Kilbeggan last time; previously ran well enough over C&D; cheekpieces added; looks stable first-string so respected.
runners.comment: Some good runs in defeat chasing; cheekpieces added; looks stable first-string; respected

runners.name: Presenting Lad
runners.spotlight: Dual chase winner but hasn't won since taking a Kilbeggan handicap hurdle in 2024; was second to Femme Magnifique at Tramore and the ground was maybe too soft at Roscommon since; could rebound and be a player.
runners.comment: The ground was maybe too soft at Roscommon last time; could rebound and be a player

runners.name: Ma Belle Etoile
runners.spotlight: Second runner for yard; bumper and hurdle winner got off the mark over fences over C&D last July; disappointing at Tramore next time; respectable run in a Flat maiden on recent return; should go well.
runners.comment: C&D winner; respectable run in a Flat maiden on recent return; should go well
runners.owner: P Haughey & P M Haughey & John Ball

runners.name: Jane Eire
runners.spotlight: Third runner for yard; maiden hurdle winner has been well beaten in three chase runs including at this track; however, her chase mark is 8lb below hurdles one, so can't be dismissed if back to her best.
runners.comment: Well beaten in three chases but her mark is 8lb below hurdles one, so can't be dismissed

runners.name: Be Fierce
runners.spotlight: Two of his three wins have come at Tramore, including his sole chase score; solid in defeat at Tramore and Punchestown the last twice; ran well enough over C&D once, so could go well.
runners.comment: Solid in defeat at Tramore and Punchestown last twice; has run well here, so could go well

runners.name: That's About Right
runners.spotlight: One of his three hurdle wins came at this track in 2024; dual chase winner afterwards; mostly below form over fences last season, but this ground suits better so could put up an improved show.
runners.comment: Mostly below form over fences last season but this ground suits, so could do better

runners.name: Mic Drop
runners.spotlight: Improved on his three previous chase runs when winning a Limerick handicap last time over 2m1f; raised 7lb and in grade so will have to do more, but a capable 5lb claimer helps.
runners.comment: Limerick winner up 7lb and in grade so will have to do more, but capable 5lb claimer helps

runners.name: Mercury Mission
runners.spotlight: Two of his three hurdle wins have come at this track; solid in defeat on his last three chase runs, including over C&D; has the credentials to run well now entering handicaps.
runners.comment: Solid on his last three chase runs including over C&D; can run well now entering handicaps
runners.owner: Paul M Butler & James Patrick Byrne

runners.name: Tequila Talkin'
runners.spotlight: Second runner for these connections; course hurdle winner over 2m4f last year, was hit and miss in that sphere after; both chase runs have been here with best run a second over C&D last time; up 2lb and in grade but can be a player.
runners.comment: Best chase run a second over C&D last time; up 2lb and in grade, but can be a player

runners.name: Party President
runners.spotlight: Dual hurdle winner for Tony Mullins last year over a similar trip to this on good ground; pulled up on stable/chase debut at Tramore behind Femme Magnifique but a solid second at Roscommon since; should run well raised 2lb.
runners.comment: Solid second at Roscommon last time on second chase start; should run well raised 2lb

runners.name: An Peann Dearg
runners.spotlight: One of two runners for these connections; best run when winning a valuable 2m1f Leopardstown handicap chase in 2025; hasn't been as good since, with best runs coming back at that track; questions after pulled-up over C&D last time.
runners.comment: Best form at Leopardstown; questions after pulled-up over C&D last time

races.race_name: Duncannon Beach Handicap Chase

runners.name: Sea Of Doubt
runners.spotlight: Good runs in two of last three hurdle starts, all at Downpatrick, winning and runner-up over 2m2f; has run well over fences before and 8lb lower mark in this sphere; looks solid.
runners.comment: Good runs in two of last three hurdles; has run well over fences before and 8lb lower mark

runners.name: Polly Poppins
runners.spotlight: Maiden and most of her best form has come at Tramore, running well on chase debut there last October; should come on from seasonal debut effort and has run well here too; considered.
runners.comment: Most of her best form has come at Tramore, but has run well here too; considered

runners.name: L'Amiral Fromentin
runners.spotlight: Maiden was third in a Limerick handicap and might have found the ground too soft at Roscommon last time; dropped 1lb and mightn't be too far away if rebounding.
runners.comment: Third at Limerick and might have found the ground too soft at Roscommon last time

runners.name: Benzine
runners.spotlight: Maiden in all spheres after 21 starts; however, has been running well enough lately; second on chase debut at Limerick and two decent hurdle runs since; chance.
runners.comment: Second on chase debut at Limerick and two decent hurdle runs since; chance

runners.name: Chosen Shant
runners.spotlight: Two hurdle wins here last year over a similar trip to this; maiden over fences but some fair efforts; poor at Roscommon last time but can do better back here.
runners.comment: Two hurdle wins here last year; poor over fences at Roscommon last time, but can do better

runners.name: Sampoet
runners.spotlight: First reserve; point winner is a maiden on the track; a decent chase run last time over 3m at Downpatrick but has run well over shorter; blinkers added.
runners.comment: Point winner is a maiden on the track; decent chase run last time over 3m; reserve

runners.name: Auntie Lacey
runners.spotlight: Point winner showed a hint of ability over hurdles; has run well enough on both chase starts; of some interest on handicap debut down in trip.
runners.comment: Point winner has run well enough on both chase starts; of some interest on handicap debut

runners.name: Figurehead
runners.spotlight: Maiden in all spheres after 46 starts; has run well in defeat at times over fences; poor over hurdles on return but would have a squeak on his best chase runs of last season.
runners.comment: Maiden has run well in defeat at times over fences; poor over hurdles on return

runners.name: Jouster
runners.spotlight: Dual hurdle winner won a Tramore beginners' chase winner in 2024 but is yet to win a handicap; has to improve his recent form.
runners.comment: Tramore beginners' chase winner in 2024 is yet to win a h'cap; has to improve recent form

runners.name: Shannon Bank
runners.spotlight: Won over hurdles at Listowel last time; previously ran well enough on debut over fences here, both over this trip; in form so has to be strongly considered.
runners.comment: Won over hurdles at Listowel last time; previously ran well over fences here; considered

runners.name: Sunwalk
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; maiden over hurdles and well beaten last twice; needs his best on chase debut after a freshening-up.
runners.comment: Maiden over hurdles and well beaten last twice; needs best on chase debut; reserve

runners.name: Tumuch
runners.spotlight: Triple point winner has shown some ability over hurdles but not so good over fences, pulled-up in a Down Royal hunter chase in March; tongue-tie on.
runners.comment: Triple point winner has shown some ability over hurdles but not so good over fences

runners.name: Ahellofaman
runners.spotlight: Regressive over hurdles lately and well beaten sole chase start, so is not high on the list.
runners.comment: Regressive over hurdles lately and well beaten sole chase start, so not high on the list

runners.name: Quinn The Eskimo
runners.spotlight: Second runner for yard; hurdle winner made a decent chase debut last year but not as good after; well beaten on return but is capable of better.
runners.comment: Decent chase debut last year; well beaten on return but is capable of better

runners.name: Kingdom Calling
runners.spotlight: Shock Listowel maiden hurdle winner in 2024; hit and miss since, plus was well beaten on chase debut last time at Tramore, but can do better.
runners.comment: Hurdle winner was well beaten on chase debut last time, but can do better

runners.name: Miss Oreo
runners.spotlight: Maiden over fences; generally regressive over hurdles since a 2023 maiden hurdle win; broke blood vessels on return at Killarney last time and has to bounce back.
runners.comment: Broke blood vessels on return at Killarney last time and has to bounce back

races.race_name: Adare Manor Opportunity Handicap Chase

runners.name: Leave In Secret
runners.spotlight: Won a Ballinrobe maiden hurdle and a Galway handicap over 2m last year; some good chase runs in defeat since, including here; can improve from return effort; major step up in trip, but a player if staying.
runners.comment: Two hurdle wins over 2m; some good chase runs; steps up in trip and a player if staying

runners.name: Malton Groove
runners.spotlight: Maiden in all spheres after 32 starts but was second at Downpatrick last time over a similar trip; raised 1lb but is up in grade today, so needs a bit more.
runners.comment: Second at Downpatrick last time; raised 1lb but is up in grade today, so needs a bit more

runners.name: Harbour Highway
runners.spotlight: Point and bumper winner didn't win in five hurdle starts, but off the mark over fences in a 2m5f Leopardstown handicap in March; twice runner-up since including over 3m; softer ground might not have suited at Roscommon last time, and this should suit better with cheekpieces retained.
runners.comment: Softer ground might not have suited at Roscommon last time and this should suit better

runners.name: Sammy Smart
runners.spotlight: A winner over hurdles and fences at Punchestown up to 3m; some good runs in defeat in the spring before a lesser Ballinrobe effort; unlucky to unseat early last time; can be a player.
runners.comment: Some good runs in defeat in the spring; unlucky to unseat early last time; can be a player

runners.name: Menas Miracle
runners.spotlight: Won over hurdles at this meeting last year and added a chase win at Tramore last time over 2m6f; the runner-up then has won since, so should be capable of going well off a 5lb higher mark.
runners.comment: Course winner had the form of her Tramore chase win last time franked; can go well up 5lb

runners.name: Riggs
runners.spotlight: Won consecutive races over fences last year including here over 3m1f; best run since was back here, so could put two lesser runs behind him on return to this venue.
runners.comment: Best form has come here, so could put two lesser runs behind him back to this venue
runners.owner: Paul M Butler & James Patrick Byrne

runners.name: Secrecies Of Stone
runners.spotlight: Second runner for yard; hurdle and chase winner has lost his form since last autumn, though was hampered when pulled-up last time; needs a revival.
runners.comment: Lost form since last autumn, though was hampered when pulled-up last time; needs a revival

runners.name: Therellbguddaysyet
runners.spotlight: Point and maiden hurdle winner; was disappointing in first-time blinkers at Listowel last time when keen; cheekpieces tried today; ran well enough on sole chase start last year; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Disappointing in blinkers last time when keen; cheekpieces today; second chase start

runners.name: Duty Bound
runners.spotlight: Flat winner in UK and has won twice over hurdles at 2m4f for this yard; won on chasing debut at this track over 2m4f in 2024 but fell on only chase run since; poor on Flat and hurdles last two runs, but not dismissed.
runners.comment: Won on chase bow here in 2024; poor on Flat and hurdles last two runs but not dismissed
runners.owner: Desmond O'Brien & John T O'Neill & J P Flavin

runners.name: Teenage Kiss
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark over hurdles when winning a 3m handicap at this track last time; chasing debut today but did run well on sole point start, so has to be considered.
runners.comment: Hurdle winner here last time; chasing debut today but did run well on sole point start

runners.name: Bohemian Bay
runners.spotlight: Some ability in points but not much in six hurdle starts and will have to do better now tackling fences.
runners.comment: Not much promise in six hurdle starts and will have to do better now tackling fences

runners.name: Rue Taylor
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner in UK and both hurdle wins for this yard have come at this track up to 3m; regressive over hurdles last autumn and well beaten in two beginners' chases; has to return to form.
runners.comment: Dual hurdle winner here but well beaten in two beginners' chases; has to return to form

races.race_name: Cottage Autism Network Mares Beginners Chase

runners.name: Pampar Lady
runners.spotlight: Rattled up a hat-trick over hurdles last summer, at up to 2m3f; after seven months off, ran well on chase debut at Tramore, upsides Future Prospect when that one departed two out, then just got tired and finished behind Beauforts Storm; jumping was a bit novicey then, so should improve for the experience and the run.
runners.comment: Ran well on chase debut at Tramore; should improve for the experience and the run

runners.name: Farfromnowhere
runners.spotlight: Point winner and best of these over hurdles; ran creditably against some of last season's leading novice mares in two chase spins in midwinter; off since finishing down the field in the Martin Pipe, but goes well fresh and at her local track.
runners.comment: Ran respectably against some of last season's leading novice mares in two chase spins

runners.name: Lady Bluebird
runners.spotlight: Back to form over hurdles in recent months, but she's a long way inferior to several of these in that sphere and it's late in the day to be going chasing; best watched.
runners.comment: Long way inferior to several of these over hurdles and late in the day to be going chasing

runners.name: Swing Davis
runners.spotlight: Point and maiden hurdle winner; jumped poorly in pair of midwinter chase outings against geldings; should come on from respectable hurdles run last month and now tried in blinkers.
runners.comment: Jumped poorly in pair of midwinter chase outings against geldings; blinkered

runners.name: Colleen Danu
runners.spotlight: Three wins in handicap hurdles, including repeat victory in Clonmel contest over this trip last month; some smart chasers further back in her pedigree, but she's entitled to improve for this first run over fences.
runners.comment: Three wins in handicap hurdles and some smart chasers further back in her pedigree

runners.name: Aruntothequeen
runners.spotlight: Listed bumper winner and disappointing that she hasn't managed to win in eight starts over hurdles; dam was a smart chaser, she loves good ground and has O'Keeffe's assistance, so not without positives for chase debut.
runners.comment: Disappointing over hurdles but dam was a smart chaser and she has O'Keeffe's assistance
runners.owner: J P O'Flaherty & Mark E Phelan

runners.name: Future Prospect
runners.spotlight: Point winner was useful in bumpers and over hurdles; can be keen and was so when without hood for chase debut at Tramore; 4-9 fav then, raced prominently and jumped well in the main, but went left at a few fences and looked vulnerable when taking a heavy fall two out; not crying out for this extra half-mile, but reapplied hood should help.
runners.comment: In front but looked vulnerable when taking a heavy fall two out at Tramore; up in trip

runners.name: Beauforts Storm
runners.spotlight: Useful hurdler with placed form in Listed and Graded company as a novice; much improved on fourth chase start at Tramore when close second, around 2l in front of Pampar Lady in race in which Future Prospect departed two out; not sure to appreciate this extra half-mile.
runners.comment: Much improved at Tramore when close second, around 2l in front of Pampar Lady

races.race_name: Support The Irish Injured Jockeys Fund Beginners Chase

runners.name: In The Trenches
runners.spotlight: Long-standing maiden finally got it done when winning readily over hurdles last month; second in three of 13 chase starts, and some fair runs over fences without being placed in April/May; these types can kick on once they get their heads in front, and Donoghue prefers him to Sophia Rose.
runners.comment: Finally got it done when winning over hurdles last month; second in three of 13 chase runs

runners.name: Rockstown Girl
runners.spotlight: Three wins over hurdles; fair bit of experience over fences and was second of 16 in C&D handicap in April; had In The Trenches behind when third to a smart mare at Kilbeggan and latest hurdling third was fine; new rider aboard.
runners.comment: Three wins over hurdles; fair bit of experience over fences and was second in C&D handicap

runners.name: Sophia Rose
runners.spotlight: Three seconds from eight starts over fences, and just run out of it close home in Killarney handicap seven weeks ago; mark went up 4lb to 106 after that; apparent second-string for Gavin Cromwell, but has her chance.
runners.comment: Three seconds from eight starts over fences; just run out of it close home in Killarney

runners.name: Rockonliam
runners.spotlight: Hurdles mark now 109 after Listowel handicap win over a furlong farther (good to yielding), his first start for five months; they were mulling over staying over flights a bit longer, so interesting that the brother to Scottish National winner Captain Cody is now deemed ready for chasing; ran well in sole 4yo point.
runners.comment: Listowel hurdle winner is brother to Scottish National winner Captain Cody; chase debut

runners.name: Juke Hill
runners.spotlight: Second in a point; modest enough over hurdles, but would have needed latest run in Punchestown handicap where he ran well to a point; cheekpieces swapped for blinkers on chase debutant from decent family.
runners.comment: Second in a point; modest enough over hurdles, but would have needed latest run; blinkers

runners.name: Ned In The Park
runners.spotlight: Prominent racer over hurdles, won 2m4f handicap at Clonmel in April and fair third back there last time; chase debut.
runners.comment: Prominent racer over hurdles, won 2m4f handicap at Clonmel in April; chase debut

runners.name: Burru
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; pair of 2m4f handicap wins over hurdles and twice placed in Roscommon handicap chases last summer; has a chance.
runners.comment: Pair of 2m4f wins over hurdles, twice placed in Roscommon handicap chases; second reserve

runners.name: Listentillitellyea
runners.spotlight: First reserve; plenty of chase experience and reached mark of 121 last year but poor in three runs this year and good ground was said to be against him in two of those.
runners.comment: Poor in three runs this year and good ground is said to be against him; first reserve

runners.name: Ballyearl
runners.spotlight: Won a Tipperary maiden hurdle last year, and met trouble in running when held twice afterwards; tongue-tie added to hood for chase debut; bred for this game but is stable second-string and has been off nearly a year.
runners.comment: Won a Tipperary maiden hurdle last year, bred for this game but is stable second-string

runners.name: Kashchei
runners.spotlight: Won non-event over hurdles by a street at Kelso in May, beating only other rival to stand up; still, he just gets in here on the ceiling rating and half-brother and dam won over fences; chase debut.
runners.comment: Won non-event over hurdles by a street at Kelso; half-brother and dam won over fences

runners.name: Clody Diamond
runners.spotlight: Won a point at four; second in maiden hurdle here last year and fair handicap run when last seen in January; hooded for chase debut.
runners.comment: Won a point at four; second in maiden hurdle here last year;  hooded for chase debut

runners.name: Silver Salute
runners.spotlight: Twice second from seven starts in points; first hurdles run at Leopardstown seemed promising but hasn't built on that; chase debut.
runners.comment: Twice second in points; first hurdles run seemed promising but hasn't built on that

runners.name: Drop A Threat
runners.spotlight: Easy point winner for Donnchadh Doyle but has been disappointing under rules, including over fences last thrice, weakening each time; heading for the sales soon, one would imagine.
runners.comment: Point winner has been disappointing under rules, including over fences last thrice

runners.name: Malbas
runners.spotlight: Did well over hurdles last year, winning a couple of handicaps; unfancied and has shown little in three starts this year, including chase debut in a Limerick handicap.
runners.comment: Shown little in three starts this year, including chase debut in a Limerick handicap

runners.name: Jetovango
runners.spotlight: Modest pointer and hurdler is having first run over regulation fences; sure to be a big price.
runners.comment: Modest pointer and hurdler is having first run over regulation fences; big outsider

runners.name: Paul Collins
runners.spotlight: Out of form over hurdles since winning off 107 at Galway in September; only 10s for Listowel handicap on chase debut the time before, but made no impact; best watched after a break.
runners.comment: Out of form over hurdles since winning off 107 at Galway in September; second chase run

races.race_name: Racing Again July 3rd Rated Novice Chase

runners.name: Londonofficecallin
runners.spotlight: Cracking dual-purpose 9yo has won four hurdles and gained most valuable of six Flat wins at Cork in May; lacks a bit of scope for this game, as shown when a long way off Chichester Park at Kilbeggan; better at the latter venue since, but was still low and left at a few fences.
runners.comment: Lacks a bit of scope for this game; better at Kilbeggan latest, but low and left at a few

runners.name: Costacurta
runners.spotlight: A 120-rated hurdler, so just sneaks in here, and won good-ground maiden over this trip last summer; after seven months off, was on his head the whole way and jumped novicey and left when third on chase debut at Tramore; going back up in trip at a left-handed track looks a smart move; related to winning chasers and likely to improve.
runners.comment: Jumped novicey and left when third on chase debut at Tramore; longer trip will help

runners.name: Maxi Mac Gold
runners.spotlight: Has had six chase runs, best effort when second over 3m2f at Fairyhouse in January (jumped left); not far off the placed horses when fourth to an easy winner over 2m6f last time; has had a break and runs well fresh; place claims.
runners.comment: Six chase runs, best effort when second over 3m2f at Fairyhouse in January; place claims

runners.name: Reiki Revolution
runners.spotlight: Bumper and 2m4f maiden hurdle winner; never competitive against smart rivals in first two chases, but was running with some credit when exiting two out both times; a clear round the obvious priority now.
runners.comment: Running with some credit when exiting two out in both chases; clear round the priority

runners.name: Garnacho
runners.spotlight: Fair good-ground hurdler over around 2m4f, easy winner at Bellewstown last summer and comes here on the back of a good run at Listowel; nicely-bred sort goes chasing now under regular rider.
runners.comment: Comes here on the back of a good run at Listowel; goes chasing now under regular rider

runners.name: Howdoyalikeherhome
runners.spotlight: Fair hurdler's sole win was over 2m6f and stays 3m; second in a couple of points, but jumping lacked conviction when long way behind Chichester Park at Kilbeggan last month on first outing since August.
runners.comment: Jumping lacked conviction when long way behind Chichester Park at Kilbeggan last month

runners.name: Chichester Park
runners.spotlight: Won on fifth start in points and decent hurdler ran well in Punchestown festival handicap; little wrong with his chase debut second at Kilbeggan, where he was fairly nimble when in close to his fences.
runners.comment: Little wrong with his chase debut second at Kilbeggan where he was fairly nimble

runners.name: Mister Pink
runners.spotlight: Good-ground bumper and maiden hurdle winner for Willie Mullins; four chase runs for this yard have been okay; well held in a handicap last time, albeit that was a decent contest and the experience will stand to him back in a novice.
runners.comment: Well held in a decent handicap last time, but the experience will stand to him

runners.name: Lawful Ruler
runners.spotlight: 1-10 in points and won 3m hunter chase at Cork in April; well beaten in Tramore hunter chase two weeks later; likely to struggle in this company.
runners.comment: Won 3m hunter chase at Cork in April, well beaten in Tramore hunter chase two weeks later

runners.name: Ephesus
runners.spotlight: Galileo 5yo is a dual Flat winner and landed Limerick maiden hurdle last year; would have needed first run of the year when never a factor on chase debut at Kilbeggan; jumped okay but probably best watched here.
runners.comment: Never a factor on chase debut at Kilbeggan; jumped okay but probably best watched here

races.race_name: Melissa, Arabella And Oriana Hawthorne Handicap

runners.name: Keats House
runners.spotlight: Both wins in the second half of 2025, first time when beating Perfidia at Beverley (8.4f) and second time here (1m); one to note given he seems to be building up to a bigger effort this time around.
runners.comment: One to note given he seems to be building up to a bigger effort

runners.name: Perfidia
runners.spotlight: Showed his tactical versatility when scoring over the extended mile at Hamilton in May (held up) and Nottingham on Thursday (made all); is unpenalised for latest success given it came in an apprentice race and Jake Dickson keeps the ride; obvious claims.
runners.comment: Obvious claims given he's unpenalised for Thursday's win (made all)

runners.name: Without Delay
runners.spotlight: Likes it here but just one run of note since returning from a third bout of wind surgery in April and returning to this longer trip isn't a positive.
runners.comment: Mostly out of form and returning to this longer trip isn't a positive

runners.name: Approaching Dawn
runners.spotlight: Yet to master the start; it was seventh-time lucky over 1m here this month and quickly bounced back from a soft-ground disappointment to be third under same conditions as win last week; extra furlong here gives him more time to catch up; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Yet to master the start but extra furlong gives him more time to catch up
runners.owner: D P Van Der Hoeven & D G Pryde

runners.name: Mayor Of Maghera
runners.spotlight: Two good runs from two for this yard, particularly when finding just a very good yardstick and a next-time-out winner too strong over C&D first time; in the mix under Daniel Tudhope, especially if first-time cheekpieces give him a lift.
runners.comment: Two good runs from two for this yard; in the mix under Daniel Tudhope

runners.name: Bay Dream Believer
runners.spotlight: Landed hat-trick last August and hit the target at Pontefract two runs ago (all 1m2f); follow-up effort at Ripon, where she's won three times, was bitterly disappointing 13 days ago and her favoured hold-up tactics aren't easy to execute around here.
runners.comment: Poor follow-up attempt and coming with a late run at this track isn't easy

runners.name: Second Fiddle
runners.spotlight: Form of her yard is a positive but she hasn't shown much of late and a revival is more likely to come on AW.
runners.comment: Hasn't shown much of late and a revival is more likely to come on AW

races.race_name: EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C And D) (EBF Restricted Qualifier) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: Turnstile
runners.spotlight: 0-6 but he has run with great credit in selling company on his last two starts; conditions will suit and he's a solid candidate once again.
runners.comment: 0-6 but he has run well in selling company on his last two starts; solid candidate

runners.name: Heffernan Kingdom
runners.spotlight: Promise in two runs this summer, showing better form when third of nine in a 5.5f Wetherby fillies' novice 26 days ago; place claims at least in this field.
runners.comment: Second run was better than her debut; more to come and she holds place claims at least

runners.name: Dadawada
runners.spotlight: 15,000euros foal, 8,000gns yearling, 18,000gns breeze-up 2yo; fourth foal; half-sister to US 6f turf winner Bright Eye Bungle; dam unplaced 7f/1m (RPR 45), half-sister to useful winners Titus Shadow (5f/6f Group 3), Orpen Shadow (6f 2yo Group 3) and Choisir Shadow (6f Listed); starts out at a realistic level and she is worth a market check.
runners.comment: 18,000gns breeze-up 2yo; half-sister to a 6f winner in the US; worth a market check

runners.name: Lexington Boom
runners.spotlight: 4,000gns foal, 23,000euros yearling; first foal of a maiden half-sister to winners Clearpoint and Music History; first 2yo runner of the year for the stable; market instructive.
runners.comment: 23,000euros yearling; some appeal on paper; first 2yo runner of the year for the stable

runners.name: Bear Lee
runners.spotlight: Lacked the pace to land a telling blow when fourth of nine in a 6f novice at Redcar (good ground) ten days ago; open to improvement and one to note for market confidence.
runners.comment: Well held in fourth on recent Redcar debut; open to improvement but it is needed
runners.owner: N Bradley Racing 29, D Scott & P Spencer

runners.name: Just For Monty
runners.spotlight: 50-1 and little short-term promise on recent debut at Ayr (6f, soft); different conditions today but looks best watched.
runners.comment: Well-beaten 50-1 shot on recent debut at Ayr (6f, soft); different conditions today

runners.name: Aodhan
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in 6f good-ground novices at Redcar and York; drop to 5f needs to have a big impact.
runners.comment: Well beaten in two 6f novices this month; not easily recommended

races.race_name: Join Racing TV Now Handicap

runners.name: High Degree
runners.spotlight: Sole win in 1m Ffos Las maiden as 3yo; beaten favourite in last four handicaps but soft and heavy ground no help in 2025 and he's performed with credit against rivals with potential this season, over 1m2f at Newbury and 1m at Sandown (both on good; probably effective on soft); solid chance at this intermediate trip for yard with 34% strike-rate here, 10-29.
runners.comment: Beaten favourite in last 4 handicaps but creditable shows at 1m2f and 1m this year

runners.name: Dwindling Funds
runners.spotlight: Six wins in 2025 over a range of trips (7f-1m2f) and ground; encouraging reappearance over C&D but not so good since and doubtful whether today will be the day from out of the weights.
runners.comment: Six wins in 2025; good reappearance over C&D; not so good since; out of the weights
runners.owner: D P Van Der Hoeven & D G Pryde

runners.name: Pandemonium
runners.spotlight: Started career with 7f wins in maiden and novice events (soft/good to soft) but things went haywire after, way behind in Group 3 (9-1) and on handicap debut at Newmarket (1m; 7-1); gelded since though and returned with a much better effort, losing out only late over C&D (good; first-time cheekpieces); back up 2lb; should find something similar if the mindset holds.
runners.comment: Won first 2 starts before career went haywire; good 2nd over C&D on return; bang there

runners.name: Strength Of Spirit
runners.spotlight: Three wins at 7f and 1m (on this track; good to soft) but topped those efforts when a running-on close third, just behind Pandemonium over C&D (good; same terms) this month; should have more options now he's shown he stays this far; solid chance for last year's winning yard.
runners.comment: Three wins at 7f-1m; stayed on well when close 3rd over C&D latest; solid chance
runners.owner: Hambleton Racing Ltd XXXV & E Burke

runners.name: Haayimm
runners.spotlight: Stepped up on sole 2yo run when winning Pontefract maiden (1m, good to soft) then made a spirited late dash at Hamilton (about 1m, good) to challenge one who later finished close up in the Britannia at Royal Ascot; looks sure to improve with this extra distance and likely good pace on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Won 1m maiden; strong finish when 2nd to useful one since; slightly further will suit

runners.name: Monoceros
runners.spotlight: Promising 7f form for Charlie Appleby included a Newmarket novice win; has a weights chance with Strength Of Spirit on their 1m run here (good to soft) in April, when making his yard debut, but he dropped right out over C&D since, behind Pandemonium and Strength Of Spirit; much work to do.
runners.comment: Weights chance with Strength Of Spirit on 1m April form; dropped right out over C&D since

runners.name: Two B Tanned
runners.spotlight: Front-runs; major improvement for this yard in 2025, winning five times, including over C&D; improved on those efforts when coming clear in fine style over 1m here (good) on reappearance but she ran poorly when going unpenalised at Redcar next time and now has an 8lb higher mark on her plate; probably wouldn't want much rain.
runners.comment: Front-runs; improved form when winning over 1m here on return; well below that form since

runners.name: Rochfortbridge
runners.spotlight: Successful debut over 7f at Ayr (good to soft) last July; asked some stiff questions since and although he ran some fair races in defeat, his reappearance in a conditions race on Kempton AW wasn't one of them; gelded since; may fare better now handicapping but this is competitive.
runners.comment: Highly tried after 7f debut win; well beaten on AW return; gelded since; handicap debut

runners.name: Marhaba Ghaiyyath
runners.spotlight: A good run of form last summer included a close second of 18 in a valuable 1m2f handicap at Glorious Goodwood (good; unraced on slower); however, he's failed to live up to that form since gelded and first-time cheekpieces (discarded now) may have been more of a hindrance than a help last time; capable but risky.
runners.comment: Good form last summer included close 2nd in big 1m2f handicap at Goodwood; risky now

runners.name: Thunder Wonder
runners.spotlight: Can lead; 2-2 on this course in 2025, over 1m (good to soft) and 1m1f (good) but he hasn't been at full strength this year, including when back over C&D in April; this is a drop in grade from Newmarket in May but a complete revival is up in the air; yard runs two.
runners.comment: 2-2 here in 2025; below best this year, including over C&D; down in grade from latest

races.race_name: Max Recycle Handicap

runners.name: Ramon Di Loria
runners.spotlight: 11-time winner, including C&D, but he hasn't been at his best in two turf runs this summer; returns to 7f in a modest event and a return to form could be imminent.
runners.comment: C&D winner; not at his best in two turf runs this summer; returns to 7f in a modest event

runners.name: Willowinghurn
runners.spotlight: Won twice as a 2yo, including a C&D nursery (soft) off a 7lb higher mark; only one effort of note this year and hopes rest on the new cheekpieces sparking a revival.
runners.comment: C&D winner on a good mark; yet to shine in 2026 and cheekpieces now reached for

runners.name: Monhammer
runners.spotlight: C&D winner who got back on track with third of eight in 7f classified event at Ayr ten days ago; not discounted back in handicap company.
runners.comment: C&D winner; latest Ayr third was a return to form; others still appeal more

runners.name: Classy Clarets
runners.spotlight: Has won over 7f but this year's two victories have been achieved at sprint trips; not beaten far having forced the pace at Catterick (7f, good) last week; apprentice takes off a useful 5lb today.
runners.comment: This year's wins have come over sprint trips and 7f stretched him at Catterick last week

runners.name: Sanditon
runners.spotlight: Both his wins have come on AW but he has had few chances on the grass and seems effective on good or quicker, as when third of eight at Catterick (7f) last week; in the mix with a repeat.
runners.comment: Both wins on AW but he handles turf and arrives in top form; one to take seriously

runners.name: Cotai Starlight
runners.spotlight: Her first two runs (debut came when trained by Ed Walker) were respectable but she took a backwards step at Catterick (7f, good) in April; she has undergone wind surgery prior to this handicap debut; can do better.
runners.comment: Improvement required now handicapping but she has had wind surgery and it's quite possible

runners.name: Rajstar
runners.spotlight: Won a weak 6f event at Newcastle on his handicap debut in February; not so good in either run since, especially when beaten a long way at Ripon 12 days ago on his return to turf; only of interest if backed.
runners.comment: Won a weak 6f handicap at Newcastle in February; poor form since; risky back up in trip

runners.name: Coconut Bay
runners.spotlight: Won four times last year; promising third at Ayr last month but she hasn't built on it in two subsequent outings; sneaking down the weights but others appeal more for win purposes.
runners.comment: Four wins in 2025 but she has only run to form once this year; not a solid option

runners.name: Defence Missile
runners.spotlight: 12-race maiden who hasn't offered a great deal for his current stable, including when a remote last of eight at Ayr (7f, soft) on his seasonal return 11 days ago; others much safer.
runners.comment: Poor form for current stable, including seasonal debut 11 days ago; others are much safer

races.race_name: Racing TV Profits Returned To Racing Handicap

runners.name: Son
runners.spotlight: He had dropped a long way in the weights prior to joining Tim Easterby and he has been competitive in some useful events this year, including a third of 19 behind a smart prospect at York (7f, good; did best of the prominent racers); down in grade today and he should go well.
runners.comment: Threatening for new yard and latest York 3rd came in a stronger race; good chance

runners.name: Abduction
runners.spotlight: C&D win off this mark in April; mixed since but his latest third at Ayr (6f, soft) was a sound effort and today he has the assistance of an apprentice who is good value for his 7lb claim; high on the list.
runners.comment: C&D win off this mark in April; good 3rd at Ayr latest; contender with good claimer booked

runners.name: The Gay Blade
runners.spotlight: Arrives in the form of his life, completing a turf hat-trick at Ayr (7f, soft) 11 days ago; that made it six wins from his last nine starts and he is respected despite the 6lb rise and stronger opposition.
runners.comment: Won 6 of his last 9 starts; conditions suitable; respected despite the stiffer opposition

runners.name: Iris Dancer
runners.spotlight: Has run well over C&D in the past but Hamilton is her spiritual home and she has added another two wins at that venue this summer, the latest a comfortable front-running success (6f, good to firm) in Class 6 company five days ago; 5lb higher over an extra furlong on a different track and in a higher grade so this may not be her day.
runners.comment: Hamilton specialist; won easily over 6f last week but this is a very different test

runners.name: Diamont Katie
runners.spotlight: Returned from wind surgery with a comfortable success at Pontefract (6f, good) in April; creditable runs in defeat since but she will need to pull out more to defy this mark.
runners.comment: Easy win at Pontefract in April; creditable efforts since but needs more to defy this mark
runners.owner: Middleham Park Racing Lxxiv & M Hulin

runners.name: Native Instinct
runners.spotlight: Still seeking his first handicap success and he has been beaten as favourite in both starts this year; on a dangerous mark and likely to be popular again.
runners.comment: Yet to win a handicap but on a dangerous mark and sure to be popular once more

runners.name: Gressington
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 with a 7f AW win at Newcastle; not in much form since returning from a three-month break and too much to prove for now.
runners.comment: Should be well handicapped but four runs back from a short break have been underwhelming
runners.owner: Ursa Major Racing Ltd And Partner

runners.name: Ameilya
runners.spotlight: 1m maiden win at Gowran Park in April 2024 seems a distant memory; out of sorts on Flat and over hurdles from July 2024 onwards; starts out for new stable after 399 days off with a lot to prove.
runners.comment: Regressive over hurdles and on Flat since July 2024; best watched on stable debut

races.race_name: Catherine & Charlotte Roche Handicap

runners.name: Sophiesticate
runners.spotlight: Last three wins came over C&D; hit flat spot but rallied for third under these conditions last week; obvious claims of turning the tables on winner Gemini Man given she was beaten two necks and has a 4lb pull; 7lb claimer Kyle McHugh enlisted too.
runners.comment: Beaten two necks by Gemini Man over C&D last week; has 4lb pull/7lb claimer enlisted

runners.name: On The Bubble
runners.spotlight: Winner under both codes in 2024 but hasn't convinced when going beyond 1m1f on the Flat previously.
runners.comment: Dual winner in 2024 but hasn't convinced when going beyond 1m1f on the Flat previously

runners.name: Falaise Blanc
runners.spotlight: Record of one win from 23 starts; close second at Hamilton this month but was quickly left behind by the winner when filling the same position there on Thursday; passed over.
runners.comment: 1-23; passed over given he was no match at all for the winner on Thursday

runners.name: Gemini Man
runners.spotlight: Three wins from four Flat starts in 2026 include a C&D success last week (had Sophiesticate two necks back in third); likely has more to offer at this kind of trip and could easily shrug off a 4lb penalty; yard took this last year.
runners.comment: Likely has more to offer at this kind of trip and could easily shrug off a 4lb penalty

runners.name: Storm On Jura
runners.spotlight: Unplaced all eight starts and finished weakly when tackling this kind of trip once before.
runners.comment: Unplaced all eight starts and finished weakly when tackling this kind of trip once before

runners.name: Is She Now
runners.spotlight: Landed a gamble on British/stable debut in 1m5f Ayr classified contest 11 days ago (soft), leading on the bridle 2f out before driven clear to win by 4l; today's underfoot conditions might be quicker, and this is a sharper track, but she could well be up to defying a 7lb rise.
runners.comment: Landed a gamble on British/stable debut; could well be up to defying a 7lb rise

races.race_name: Watch On Racing TV Handicap

runners.name: Wee Mary
runners.spotlight: Made all over C&D (good) at the beginning of the month and very nearly repeated the feat last week, collared only in the final strides; same mark today and another bold bid looks assured in her current mood.
runners.comment: Made all over C&D at the start of June and very nearly did it again last week; good shout

runners.name: What A Tahoo
runners.spotlight: A switch to turf has seen an upturn in her fortunes, tackling 5f for the first time when opening her account at Ripon (good) 13 days ago; raised 5lb but possible she can pull out more.
runners.comment: First 5f start when off the mark at Ripon 13 days; up 5lb but may have more to give
runners.owner: Richard Fahey Ebor Racing Club Ltd

runners.name: Data Fata Secutus
runners.spotlight: Maiden who has form claims on her close third at Redcar (5f, good to firm) last month but hasn't come close to reproducing it twice since, latterly with cheekpieces added (retained).
runners.comment: Claims on her close third at Redcar last month but well held twice since

runners.name: Sands Of Seve
runners.spotlight: Easily best run when all-the-way winner for Robert Cowell in 5f Wolverhampton handicap in March but well held all three runs since; changed hands for 2,200gns since last time and this yard switch needs to spark a resurgence.
runners.comment: Made all with ease on AW in March but well held all three runs since; new yard now

runners.name: Invincible Crown
runners.spotlight: 3-12 on AW compared to 0-17 on turf but last week's second at Nottingham (5f, good to firm) suggests he can pick up a win on the grass off this sort of mark; has to enter calculations, although any easing of the ground possibly not ideal.
runners.comment: All three wins on AW but recent Nottingham second shows he's capable on turf; player
runners.owner: Grange Park Racing VIII & Mrs R Carr

runners.name: Bonito Cavalo
runners.spotlight: Won six times on AW in the first part of 2024 but there hasn't been much to cheer about since; 0-27 on turf but has run well over C&D and the forecast rain won't bother him; still too much to prove for comfort.
runners.comment: Six AW wins in 2024 but little to cheer since; 0-27 on turf and too much to prove

runners.name: Gwen Tennyson
runners.spotlight: Improved when third in 5f Catterick novice on good to firm last month but didn't come close to backing that up when beaten just under 9l into fourth on handicap debut there (good to soft) 19 days ago; early days but hard to be confident after that.
runners.comment: Third in novice last month but remote fourth on handicap debut since

runners.name: Auntie Jo
runners.spotlight: Two 5f wins for David O'Meara at Newcastle this year, the latter achieved in smooth fashion at the beginning of this month; never a threat when fifth on stable debut at Ayr (5f, soft) ten days ago, taking turf record to 0-9; the headgear she's worn on her last ten starts is left off here.
runners.comment: Two AW wins for David O'Meara in 2026; never a threat when fifth on recent Ayr yard debut
runners.owner: Making Headway Racing And K Scott

runners.name: Sixcor
runners.spotlight: C&D scorer who ran his most encouraging race of the campaign when third (Wee Mary second) over C&D (good) eight days ago; his best form of 2025 came at this time of the year so it's possible he can build on that.
runners.comment: C&D scorer who ran better than of late when third over C&D last week; may build on that

runners.name: Hard Nut
runners.spotlight: Arrives out of sorts, finishing well behind Wee Mary and stablemate Sixcor in two outings over C&D this month.
runners.comment: Well behind Wee Mary and stablemate Sixcor in two outings over C&D this month

races.race_name: Coast Cremations Cup Classified Stakes

runners.name: Valsharah
runners.spotlight: In the frame in four of his last six starts and drops to this level for the first time, but a record of 0-25 raises doubts even in this company; visor given a go this time.
runners.comment: Record of 0-25 raises doubts even in this company; visor on

runners.name: Washington Heir
runners.spotlight: Made the most of the drop in grade when making it seventh-time lucky in a similar contest here (5f, good) three weeks ago, with three of these behind; the form is modest but he is still open to a bit more improvement; respected.
runners.comment: Made it seventh-time lucky in a similar contest here three weeks ago; respected

runners.name: Big Time Rascal
runners.spotlight: Sole win in 24 starts came in a 0-55 handicap over C&D (good) in May last year; beaten just under 2l into fourth of nine behind Washington Heir here (5f, good) three weeks ago, but may be better over this trip these days; a player despite his wide draw.
runners.comment: Sole win in 24 starts came in a 0-55 handicap over C&D in May last year; a player

runners.name: Port Hedland
runners.spotlight: Just 2-29, but comes into this off the back of a win in a similar event at Bath (5.7f, good); not the most reliable but a player if in the same form; stable also runs Washington Heir.
runners.comment: Landed a similar event at Bath last time; a player if in the same form

runners.name: Manhattan Chute
runners.spotlight: Sprung a 50-1 surprise in a 0-60 handicap at Lingfield (5f, AW) in January, but subsequent efforts suggest it may have been a flash in the pan; hard to recommend despite the drop in class; stable also runs Lesley Buckley.
runners.comment: 50-1 winner at Lingfield in January, but it may have been a flash in the pan
runners.owner: J May, R L Field, W Trenchard, J Pook

runners.name: Frankali
runners.spotlight: 0-10; placed in a couple of 7f AW handicaps at the start of the year, but has regressed since then including a modest effort in one go on turf; needs the blinkers to make a difference.
runners.comment: 0-10; placed in two AW handicaps earlier in the year, but has regressed since; blinkers on

runners.name: Saeculamation
runners.spotlight: Won at Chelmsford (6f, AW) in December and ran well in his first three starts after returning to turf in April, but not so good over C&D three weeks ago; needs to bounce back, though the drop in grade is a positive.
runners.comment: 1-16; needs to bounce back from a lesser effort, but the drop in grade is a positive

runners.name: Apple's Angel
runners.spotlight: Triple AW winner (5f/6f), but 0-9 on turf and has beaten just one rival in two starts since returning for this yard last month; look elsewhere.
runners.comment: 0-9 on turf; has beaten one rival in two starts since returning for this yard last month
runners.owner: P And Mrs L Rowe And John Turner

runners.name: Lynwood Lad
runners.spotlight: Finished behind a couple of these over 5f here (good) three weeks ago, a race he had won the previous year; more needed.
runners.comment: Finished behind a couple of these over 5f here three weeks ago; more needed

runners.name: Battle Point
runners.spotlight: Just 1-51 and his best efforts have come on the AW; hard to make a case for even at this level; stable also runs Big Time Rascal.
runners.comment: Just 1-51 and his best efforts have come on the AW; hard to make a case for

runners.name: Lesley Buckley
runners.spotlight: 0-7 and may not have achieved much when third of four in a 0-55 handicap at Lingfield (5f, good; first-time blinkers) 12 days ago; more needed, even down at this level; no headgear today.
runners.comment: 0-7; may not have achieved much when third of four at Lingfield 12 days ago; no headgear

runners.name: Coast
runners.spotlight: Winner of six races and ran well when third of nine in a 0-55 handicap here (5f, good to firm) last month, but current losing run now up to 29; drawn widest and others are more compelling.
runners.comment: Current losing run now up to 29; drawn widest and others are more compelling

races.race_name: Sheephaven Bay And Tipsytipsters Remembering Boony Handicap

runners.name: Rogue Dynasty
runners.spotlight: Sole success came at Chester (7f, good; first-time hood) just under a year ago but has finished runner-up in four of her seven starts since, the latest a narrow defeat over C&D (good) three weeks ago (third has won since); major player again from 1lb higher.
runners.comment: Just beaten over C&D three weeks ago; major player again from 1lb higher

runners.name: Antiquity
runners.spotlight: Seen more often on the AW (3-21) than on turf (1-7); back off his last winning mark, but needs to improve on his last couple of efforts.
runners.comment: Back off his last winning mark, but needs to improve on his last couple of efforts
runners.owner: P&h Moorhead, J Collins And S Piper

runners.name: Buy The Dip
runners.spotlight: Has a consistent record here (32142132224) and has shown enough since returning on the AW in April to suggest he remains in form; should go well.
runners.comment: Has a consistent record here (32142132224); should go well

runners.name: My Boy Harry
runners.spotlight: Having his 15th start of the year already; still 5lb above his last winning mark and although he has won over 1m on the AW, 7f may suit him better.
runners.comment: Still 5lb above his last winning mark; has won over 1m on AW, but 7f may suit him better

runners.name: King Of War
runners.spotlight: Ran well when third of 12 on his reappearance over C&D this month (Rogue Dynasty second), but all six wins have come over 7f including when recording his fourth course success back here eight days ago, for which he has a 4lb penalty.
runners.comment: Six wins have come over 7f including here eight days ago, for which he has a 4lb penalty

runners.name: Spirit Of Albion
runners.spotlight: 0-10; placed three times for Amanda Perrett, but heavy defeats in both starts since returning for this yard last month leave him with enough to prove; first-time blinkers replace hood.
runners.comment: 0-10; has suffered heavy defeats in both starts since returning for this yard; blinkers on

runners.name: Marsh Benham
runners.spotlight: Four wins here (three over this trip) and has dropped 2lb below his last winning mark; defied a five-month absence as a 3yo, but his reappearance efforts in the meantime suggest this first start in seven months may be needed.
runners.comment: Four wins here; 2lb below last winning mark, but first start in seven months may be needed

races.race_name: Bet Now With fairplaybet.co.uk Handicap

runners.name: Hove Ranger
runners.spotlight: Bumped into one on his handicap debut early in the year before showing the right attitude to win off 1lb lower next time (both Polytrack); didn't look to see out 1m2f on soft ground back from a break recently and holds obvious claims at this lower level.
runners.comment: Didn't see out 1m2f back from a break recently; holds obvious claims at this lower level

runners.name: Denby's Dream
runners.spotlight: Yard had a few easy winners of late, including a couple at big prices; this filly, who threatened to pick up a small handicap when the hood first went on, has had excuses the past twice and is one to consider.
runners.comment: Yard had a few easy winners of late; had excuses the past twice and is one to consider

runners.name: She's Crafty
runners.spotlight: Bumped into an improver when second at Lingfield (turf) last month but she's twice run below market expectations in 0-55 company since returned to turf; essentially a regressive maiden.
runners.comment: Twice run below market expectations in 0-55 company since returned to turf; regressive

runners.name: Aneirin's Sword
runners.spotlight: Dual AW winner early in the year, latterly off a 3lb higher mark, who's gone off the boil; this is less competitive on just his second start in 0-60 company but he's still to show he's as effective on turf (two runs to date).
runners.comment: Gone off the boil but this is less competitive on just his second start in 0-60 company

runners.name: Bennyworth
runners.spotlight: Exposed maiden who hasn't seen it out in two runs over further since switched to turf; the drop in trip should help and he's been eased 5lb but he's hard to recommend nonetheless.
runners.comment: Drop in trip should help and he's been eased 5lb but he's hard to recommend nonetheless

races.race_name: Fairplay Daily Price Boosts Handicap

runners.name: Voix De Bocelli
runners.spotlight: Both wins came over C&D in the spring of last year and well treated on his best AW form of last winter; recent efforts leave him with a bit to prove.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner in spring last year, but recent efforts leave him with a bit to prove

runners.name: Little She
runners.spotlight: 0-17 but in the frame nine times, the latest when second of 11 at Chepstow (1m2f, good to soft) 15 days ago; no reason why she shouldn't go well again.
runners.comment: 0-17 but in the frame nine times; no reason why she shouldn't go well again

runners.name: Man Is King
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who has won three times at Bath (1m/1m2f) since the start of last month, but he didn't get home when fourth of nine over C&D (good to firm) nine days ago; may not face too much competition up front so could still be dangerous.
runners.comment: Has won three times at Bath since last month; may not face too much competition up front

runners.name: Twilight Guest
runners.spotlight: Yet to fire this year and has a bit to find with Bohemian Breeze on their meeting here three weeks ago, but he is 12lb lower than when completing a hat-trick around this time last year (first two legs came over C&D); worth a second look.
runners.comment: 12lb lower than when completing a hat-trick this time last year; worth a second look

runners.name: San Francisco Bay
runners.spotlight: Just 1lb higher than when winning at Nottingham (1m2f, good) last July but he needs to improve plenty on his last few efforts.
runners.comment: 1lb above his last winning mark but he needs to improve plenty on his last few efforts

runners.name: Bohemian Breeze
runners.spotlight: Took advantage of a drop in the weights when winning a 0-55 contest by a neck over C&D three weeks ago; 5lb rise puts him on his previous winning mark and although not the easiest of rides, Tom Queally knows him well (2-8 on him); major player.
runners.comment: Won over C&D three weeks ago; 5lb rise puts him on his previous winning mark

runners.name: Annexation
runners.spotlight: At the veteran stage and has finished unplaced in his last ten starts, but now 8lb below his last winning mark; could still have a big effort in him.
runners.comment: 8lb below his last winning mark and could still have a big effort in him

runners.name: Cartwheel
runners.spotlight: Having dropped a long way in the weights she looked about ready to strike when third of eight at Leicester (1m2f, good to firm) last month, but she ran poorly back there (1m4f, good) 17 days ago; enough to prove after that.
runners.comment: Looked about ready to strike on her penultimate start but ran poorly last time

races.race_name: fairplaybet.co.uk Bet £20 Get £20 Free Bets Handicap

runners.name: Havana Mojito
runners.spotlight: Three wins over 7f/1m here; runner-up in his last two starts back at this venue including when beaten half a length by Dion Baker eight days ago; a 4lb pull plus Jack Callan's 3lb claim gives him every chance of reversing the form.
runners.comment: Beaten half a length by Dion Baker over C&D eight days ago and 4lb better off; chance

runners.name: Joycean Way
runners.spotlight: Third in his last two starts over 1m/1m2f here, but now 0-19 despite a slipping mark and not sure dropping back to this trip is the key; blinkers return.
runners.comment: 0-19 despite a slipping mark and not sure dropping back to this trip is the key
runners.owner: J Harley, T Jacobs, G Moore

runners.name: Jackson Street
runners.spotlight: Usually seen on the AW and just 2lb higher than when winning at Lingfield (7f) in December; placed just once in 11 starts on turf, though, so not hard to have reservations.
runners.comment: Usually seen on the AW and placed once in 11 starts on turf; not hard to have reservations

runners.name: Henry Tudor
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in his last two starts including on his turf debut here (6f, good to firm) last month; probably best watched until returning to 0-50 classified company.
runners.comment: Well beaten in his last two starts including on his turf debut here; probably best watched

runners.name: Prefer The Sister
runners.spotlight: Just 1-26 but has run well in both starts since joining this yard, including when a close third behind Dion Baker and Havana Mojito over C&D eight days ago (flashed tail); shouldn't be far away again.
runners.comment: 1-26; close third behind two of these over C&D eight days ago; shouldn't be far away again

runners.name: Dion Baker
runners.spotlight: Completed a hat-trick since the end of last month, including two all-the-way successes over C&D; doesn't have to lead and his 4lb penalty for his win eight days ago (three of these behind) shouldn't preclude another big performance.
runners.comment: Carries a 4lb penalty in his bid four a four-timer; another big performance likely

runners.name: Bear To Dream
runners.spotlight: Four wins over 6f/7f here; just 2lb above his last winning mark but well held in both starts since returning last month, including when behind three of these over C&D eight days ago; more needed; stable also runs Jackson Street.
runners.comment: Well held in both starts since returning last month; more needed

runners.name: Starsong
runners.spotlight: Dual course winner (5f/6f) who remains just 1lb above her last winning mark; 0-11 over this trip and further, though, so others seem more likely.
runners.comment: Dual course winner, but 0-11 over this trip and further so others seem more likely

runners.name: Almavillalobas
runners.spotlight: Out of the frame in all ten starts and impossible to make a case for.
