runners.comment: Out of the frame in all ten starts and impossible to make a case for
runners.owner: C Seeney, S Holden, Mildean , M Robinson

runners.name: Lunanova
runners.spotlight: Won three times over 1m here last summer and 5lb lower than for the most recent; hasn't threatened in her last five starts, though, and another furlong would be preferable.
runners.comment: Three wins over 1m here last summer, but hasn't threatened in her last five starts

runners.name: Ridgeway Redwing
runners.spotlight: Hasn't threatened in seven starts and needs the hood to make a big difference.
runners.comment: Hasn't threatened in seven starts and needs the hood to make a big difference

races.race_name: Yeomans Toyota Proace Handicap

runners.name: Electrocution
runners.spotlight: 1-12 on the AW, the win coming from 1lb lower at Wolverhampton (7f) in February; fair fourth of eight back there (first-time blinkers) last time, but this is a tricky track on which to make his turf debut.
runners.comment: 1-12 on the AW; this is a tricky track on which to make his turf debut

runners.name: Venetian Romance
runners.spotlight: A bit in and out in her six starts, but has shown ability as when fourth of 12 at Leicester (7f, good to firm; first-time cheekpieces) in April; now 5lb lower and would have a good chance if rediscovering that form.
runners.comment: Fourth of 12 at Leicester in April; now 5lb lower; good chance if rediscovering that form

runners.name: Norcross Brow
runners.spotlight: Bounced back to form when third of seven on his stable debut at Newmarket (1m, good to firm) last Friday; should go well again.
runners.comment: Third of seven on his stable debut at Newmarket last Friday; should go well again

runners.name: Miss Starlet
runners.spotlight: Showed ability in four starts last year, but not built on it in three outings since returning last month; down another 3lb but best watched for now.
runners.comment: Modest in three outings since returning last month; down another 3lb but best watched

runners.name: Pimentel
runners.spotlight: Won a nursery over C&D (good to firm; first time cheekpieces) last September and was returning from 166 days off when a clear second behind an easy winner at Chepstow (6f, good to firm) on Saturday (taking her record under Ashley Lewis to 122); return to this trip should suit; respected.
runners.comment: C&D winner; runner-up at Chepstow on Saturday; return to 7f should suit

runners.name: Credit Forgedd It
runners.spotlight: Best effort in eight starts for Richard Hannon when beaten a neck in a Wolverhampton nursery (8.6f, AW) in December; makes his stable debut after five months off having changed hands for 6,000gns in February; market helpful.
runners.comment: Just beaten at Wolverhampton in December; stable debut after five months off; watch market

races.race_name: Irish EBF Auction Series Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: Sea Of Rain
runners.spotlight: Beaten favourite over C&D on debut, beaten 2.5l into fourth of 8 behind Arizona Raider; may recover losses now having been denied by a nose at Leopardstown 12 days ago; cheekpieces go on.
runners.comment: Beaten fav' over C&D, may recover losses having gone close at Leopardstown; cheekpieces on

runners.name: Seaxburh
runners.spotlight: Good pedigree, dam won the Rockfel Stakes, out of Oaks/Irish 1,000 Guineas winner Imagine; not beaten far when fifth of eight over C&D on debut.
runners.comment: Not beaten far when fifth of eight over C&D on debut, champion deserts for Ballyskeagh

runners.name: Nando Royale
runners.spotlight: Noted staying on when sixth of 17 at Fairyhouse and fifth-placed horse won at Naas last week.
runners.comment: Noted staying on when sixth of 17 at Fairyhouse, fifth-placed horse won at Naas last week
runners.owner: Kiva Cromwell & Coragh Bloodstock Limited & Fergus Grimes

runners.name: Oristown Boy
runners.spotlight: Had an excuse on debut; gave an encouraging display when fourth over this trip on soft ground at Down Royal.
runners.comment: Had an excuse on debut, gave an encouraging display when fourth at Down Royal
runners.owner: Ms Lisa Sheridan & Patrick M O'Brien & Larry Murray

runners.name: Buck Jones
runners.spotlight: E7,000 yearling; second foal; half-brother to a winner in Spain; dam unraced half-sister to two winners; speedy pedigree.
runners.comment: Half-brother to a winner in Spain, dam unraced half-sister to two winners, speedy pedigree

runners.name: Whosinterviewinwho
runners.spotlight: E10,000 yearling; first foal; dam placed on AW (RPR 58), half-sister to 1m1f winner Searcog; apprentice aboard with a senior rider on the stable's September Duke.
runners.comment: Inns Of Court colt, apprentice aboard with a senior rider on the stable's September Duke

runners.name: Jezzie Bea
runners.spotlight: E20,000 yearling; fourth foal; half-sister to a US 7f dirt winner; dam a Canadian stakes winner; good ground should suit.
runners.comment: Half-sister to a US 7f dirt winner; dam a Canadian stakes winner, good ground should suit

runners.name: September Duke
runners.spotlight: E25,000 yearling; fifth foal; half-brother by Sergei Prokofiev to two winners; dam a low-rated maiden, half-sister to a US Grade 3 winner.
runners.comment: Half-brother by Sergei Prokofiev to two winners, dam a low-rated maiden

runners.name: Ballyskeagh
runners.spotlight: E42,000 yearling; fourth foal; half-sister by Gleneagles to two winners; dam ran only twice, out of half-sister to Derby winner Ruler Of The World.
runners.comment: Half-sister by Gleneagles to two winners, rider was on Seaxburh first time out,

runners.name: Magna Fortuna
runners.spotlight: Dropped away at Fairyhouse, finished behind Nando Royale and Banks Of The Boyne.
runners.comment: Dropped away at Fairyhouse, finished behind Nando Royale and Banks Of The Boyne

runners.name: Peckedbytheparrott
runners.spotlight: Faced a very stiff task when introduced in a six-runner Curragh event in which Aidan O'Brien and Robson De Aguiar were responsible for the first three; of some interest in this grade, but likely to need more experience.
runners.comment: Faced a very stiff task when introduced at the Curragh, will be more at home in this grade

runners.name: Damsel In Distress
runners.spotlight: Looked in need of further when staying on in a Listowel maiden over an extended 6f; beaten 14l in fifth, considerable improvement needed.
runners.comment: Looked in need of further when staying on over extended 6f on debut, beaten 14l in fifth

runners.name: Docks
runners.spotlight: Lost ground at the start and never recovered on debut at Leopardstown; first reserve.
runners.comment: First reserve, lost ground at the start and never recovered on debut at Leopardstown

runners.name: Banks Of The Boyne
runners.spotlight: Slowly away on debut at Fairyhouse; mid-field finish was respectable in the circumstances, 2 1/2l behind sixth-placed Nando Royale.
runners.comment: Slowly away on debut at Fairyhouse, mid-field finish was respectable in the circumstances

runners.name: Supreme Act
runners.spotlight: Never in contention first time out at Down Royal 11 days ago; third reserve.
runners.comment: Third reserve, never in contention first time out at Down Royal 11 days ago

runners.name: Elyatta
runners.spotlight: Never in contention on debut in a 6f maiden at Fairyhouse; big price then despite having Colin Keane in the saddle; likely to need more experience.
runners.comment: Never in contention on debut at Fairyhouse, likely to need more experience

runners.name: Caoch The Piper
runners.spotlight: Finished behind her stablemate Supreme Act when tailed off at Down Royal; second reserve.
runners.comment: Second reserve, finished behind her stablemate Supreme Act when tailed off at Down Royal

runners.name: Cochise County
runners.spotlight: Has finished in front of only two of his 16 rivals in two C&D appearances.
runners.comment: Has finished in front of only two of his 16 rivals in two C&D appearances
runners.owner: Exors of the Late J C Fleming

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden

runners.name: Star Of Beauty
runners.spotlight: Rated 77; some solid efforts in defeat, including third of 13 in a Naas maiden last August (6f, good); bounced back from a disappointing handicap run at the Curragh when second in a C&D claimer; made most of the running at Gowran; not guaranteed to uphold form with the less exposed Elusive Echo.
runners.comment: Fair placed form, not guaranteed to uphold Gowran form with the less exposed Elusive Echo
runners.owner: David K Kelly & Mrs Pauline Condon

runners.name: Bofa Beach
runners.spotlight: Plenty of debut promise when fourth in a 7f Curragh maiden last summer; out of her depth in the Goffs Million on her only other juvenile start; began the season with a satisfactory third over 1m at Gowran; not as good over a longer trip on easier ground at the same venue last time.
runners.comment: Satisfactory third over 1m at Gowran, not as good over a longer trip theere last time

runners.name: Almeiyda
runners.spotlight: Fair fourth over 1m at Leopardstown on the second of two starts at two; appeared not to see out the extended 1m1f at Gowran eight weeks ago; 72-rated filly needs to find a little extra to win a maiden.
runners.comment: Two fourth placings to her name, rated 72, needs to find a little extra to win a maiden
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan

runners.name: Elusive Echo
runners.spotlight: First home in a barrier trial at Naas; wore a tongue-tie (retained) on debut in a 1m Gowran maiden early this month, raced prominently behind Star Of Beauty before the pair were overtaken by the favourite Amenita; fair chance of reversing form with the more exposed runner-up.
runners.comment: Promising third at Gowran, fair chance of reversing form with runner-up Star Of Beauty

runners.name: Moon Aura
runners.spotlight: Fourth in a claimer at Ballinrobe; unplaced on handicap debut at Gowran; official rating of 64 implies she needs to improve considerably to win a maiden; checkpieces added.
runners.comment: Official rating of 64 implies she needs to improve considerably to win a maiden

runners.name: Miss Moore Cen
runners.spotlight: Midfield on debut at the Curragh (6f, soft); not a bad effort when fifth of 14 at Down Royal (7f, good); needs to raise her game.
runners.comment: Not a bad effort when fifth of 14 at Down Royal (7f, good), needs to raise her game

runners.name: Wildlife
runners.spotlight: Looked raw when finishing at the back at Gowran; likely to need more experience.
runners.comment: Looked raw when finishing at the back at Gowran, likely to need more experience

runners.name: Spring Sprinter
runners.spotlight: Unplaced in two Gowran outings; held by Star Of Beauty and Elusive Echo; more likely a handicap prospect after this.
runners.comment: Held by Star Of Beauty and Elusive Echo, more likely a handicap prospect after this

runners.name: Black Chicken
runners.spotlight: Failed to make an impact on debut at Gowran; safely held by Elusive Echo on that running.
runners.comment: Failed to make an impact on debut at Gowran, safely held by Elusive Echo on that running

runners.name: Ebony Reign
runners.spotlight: Down the field at Leopardstown, tailed off at the Curragh; tongue-tie tried now.
runners.comment: Down the field at Leopardstown, tailed off at the Curragh, tongue-tie tried now

runners.name: Haroussa
runners.spotlight: 6-1 on debut at Navan; failed to make much impression in a maiden won by the promising Sparan Nua; likely to be capable of better; dam, a half-sister to Derby winner Harzand, was a Group 3 winner over 7f at two.
runners.comment: Only 6-1 on debut at Navan, failed to make much impression, likely to be capable of better
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan

runners.name: Reap The Rewards
runners.spotlight: No threat on heavy at the Curragh in March; may be seen to better effect on a sound surface; cheekpieces on and champion jockey aboard.
runners.comment: No threat on heavy on debut, may be seen to better effect on a sound surface, cheekpieces

runners.name: Ellens Bay
runners.spotlight: Has struggled in two Dundalk outings; safe to leave out of calculations.
runners.comment: Has struggled in two Dundalk outings, safe to leave out of calculations

runners.name: Storm Petrel
runners.spotlight: Down the field at Naas on her only start at two; from a small Cork stable that had a runner placed at 50-1 at the Curragh last Friday.
runners.comment: Ran once at two, small Cork stable had a runner placed at 50-1 at the Curragh last Friday

races.race_name: Jimmy Murray Agri Services Handicap

runners.name: Methgal
runners.spotlight: Made the breakthrough in a 7f Dundalk handicap in March; followed that with two seconds (at Dundalk and on turf at Leopardstown); below his best at the Curragh and Listowel, before returning to form with a Limerick fourth.
runners.comment: Below his best at the Curragh and Listowel before returning to form with a Limerick fourth

runners.name: Shahada Ace
runners.spotlight: Won a handicap at Tipperary last summer; has failed to make an impact in three outings this season, needs to show signs of revival.
runners.comment: Has failed to make an impact in three outings this season, needs to show signs of revival

runners.name: Queen Of Newark
runners.spotlight: Weak form last season; never involved in 5f races in her first two handicap starts; fine effort when placed at 66-1 in a C&D claimer; limitations underlined when she was sent back into handicap company at Down Royal.
runners.comment: Fine effort when placed at 66-1 in a C&D claimer, limitations evident in handicap company

runners.name: The Blue Panther
runners.spotlight: Fifth career win when revelling in the mud at Listowel (7f, heavy) last September; out of form in two runs this season.
runners.comment: Gained a fifth career win at Listowel last autumn, out of form in two runs this season
runners.owner: Ms Julianne McNally & Colette McNally & D McNally

runners.name: Blue Anthem
runners.spotlight: Three wins for George Boughey (two on AW); five outings for this yard provide little encouragement; however, now in 0-60 company for the first time (has won off 70) and may be competitive.
runners.comment: Five Irish outings provide limited encouragement, but may be competitive in this grade

runners.name: Eichan San
runners.spotlight: Has failed to reach the first three in 11 attempts; has shown his best form this year in two Curragh outings; first-time blinkers; third reserve.
runners.comment: Third reserve, has failed to reach the first three in 11 attempts, first-time blinkers

runners.name: Brains
runners.spotlight: Veteran winner of 14 races (11 on AW); shaped well in his opening two turf runs this year; second over 7f at Leopardstown (yielding to soft) and fourth over C&D (good); a bit below that level at Gowran on latest.
runners.comment: Veteran winner of 14 races, has run creditably in two of his three turf starts this year

runners.name: Selective Power
runners.spotlight: C&D winner on soft last autumn, the most recent of four wins; pleasing run when fourth at Gowran recently; first reserve.
runners.comment: First reserve, C&D winner on soft last autumn, pleasing run when fourth at Gowran recently

runners.name: Medieval Night
runners.spotlight: Second in a 21-runner 1m1f contest at Leopardstown last August when trained by Dermot Weld; ran his best race so far for this stable when third over same course and trip recently.
runners.comment: Maiden who ran his best race for the stable so far when third at Leopardstown recently

runners.name: Polanco
runners.spotlight: No sign of winning potential in maidens last year; has struggled in two handicap attempts; tongue-tie on now
runners.comment: No sign of winning potential in maidens last year, has struggled in two handicap attempts.

runners.name: Alandownunder
runners.spotlight: Weak form in maidens; down the field in handicaps over C&D and at Fairyhouse; needs to improve with blinkers on.
runners.comment: Weak form in maidens, down the field in two handicaps, needs to improve with blinkers on

runners.name: Givehertilxmas
runners.spotlight: Ended last season with two fair runs on turf, notably when second over 1m at Bellewstown; progress not sustained on AW; hard to fancy on her first run since January.
runners.comment: Ended last season with two fair runs on turf, progress not sustained on AW; hard to fancy

runners.name: Zheleznaya Dama
runners.spotlight: Not without a chance on her best juvenile form, namely a third placing in a 7f Down Royal nursery; saddle slipped on her reappearance at Cork; second reserve.
runners.comment: Second reserve, saddle slipped at Cork, not without a chance on her best juvenile form

runners.name: La La Lucrative
runners.spotlight: Bolted up over 7f at Listowel on seasonal debut; raised 13lb for that, well held in mid-field at Fairyhouse a fortnight later; won over C&D early last season.
runners.comment: Raised 13lb for Listowel win, well held in mid-field at Fairyhouse a fortnight later;

runners.name: He's Holding Court
runners.spotlight: First time in the frame when fourth over C&D in May, further progress is feasible.
runners.comment: First time in the frame when fourth over C&D on latest, further progress is feasible

runners.name: Toy Soldier
runners.spotlight: Won a maiden and a claimer in Britain; poor form for this yard until 2l fourth of 15 at Killarney last month; backed that up with a second placing at Down Royal; strong contender.
runners.comment: Two wins in Britain, weak Irish form until his two most recent starts, strong contender

runners.name: Misava
runners.spotlight: Two moderate runs at two; in rear over C&D a year ago on her only start at three; hooded.
runners.comment: Two moderate runs at two, in rear over C&D a year ago on her only start at three; hooded

runners.name: Snap Decision
runners.spotlight: Showed next to nothing in three 7f AW maidens; made turf debut over 5f at Cork; not disgraced, bearing in mind that his rider had to cope with a slipped saddle; could be a dark horse on handicap debut
runners.comment: Not disgraced at Cork bearing in mind that his rider had to cope with a slipped saddle

races.race_name: Conor Cassidy Car Sales Handicap

runners.name: Nermal
runners.spotlight: Consistent form in maidens at 1m-10.5f on the AW and on good and yielding ground; opening mark of 72 may be on the lenient side and he's interesting on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Consistent form in maidens, opening mark of 72 may be lenient, interesting h'cap debutant

runners.name: Freedom And Light
runners.spotlight: Best run in maidens a 9l fourth at Leopardstown (1m2f, yielding) in April; handicap debutante not obviously well treated, but worth checking in the market.
runners.comment: Modest form in maidens, handicap debutante not obviously well treated but check market

runners.name: Rapide Vega
runners.spotlight: Promise in maidens at 7f-1m on soft ground; soundly beaten in first two handicaps, his latest second at Gowran (9.5f, yielding) a remote one; eased 1lb and cheekpieces go on; more needed.
runners.comment: Promise in maidens, soundly beaten in first two handicaps, cheekpieces on, needs more

runners.name: Ella's Gold
runners.spotlight: Knocking on the door, runner-up in three of last four starts; however, all three of those runs came on rain-softened ground and concerning that her three previous runs on good ground have resulted in heavy defeats.
runners.comment: Runner up three of last four starts, all on soft, good ground form a big concern

runners.name: Honouramongthieves
runners.spotlight: 0-7; best run came when a 5l second of 8 in a Killarney maiden last October (1m, sft-hvy); shaped promisingly enough when denied a clear run back there in May (1m3f, good), but needs to rebound from dire effort at Newbury last time.
runners.comment: Best runs at Killarney, dire effort at Newbury on latest, needs to bounce back

runners.name: Dont Get Me Wrong
runners.spotlight: AW claimer winner at 7f last November; stayed on late when ninth of 14 over 7.5f (good) here on seasonal return and handicap debut last month, beaten 7.5l; not bred for this far, but worth a go at this trip on the evidence of that last run; could outrun likely odds.
runners.comment: AW winner, kept on over 7.5f here latest, worth a go at this trip, may outrun likely odds

runners.name: Vantage Code
runners.spotlight: Ordinary form in maidens and hasn't made an impact in first two handicaps; only midfield when stepped up to 1m2f at Down Royal latest; eased 4lb but others make more appeal.
runners.comment: No impact in first two handicaps, eased 4lb for midfield finish latest, others preferred

runners.name: Second Line
runners.spotlight: Didn't achieve much in four maiden starts at 7f-12.5f on variety of ground; blinkers go on for handicap debut; can only be watched.
runners.comment: Didn't cut much ice in maidens, blinkers on for handicap debut, likely best watched

runners.name: Adel
runners.spotlight: Left previous modest form behind to land a 1m4f (yielding) handicap here on latest start in May; the step up in trip was offered as the reason for the improvement in form, so the drop back in distance here may not be ideal; raised 6lb and quicker ground a worry too in this deeper race.
runners.comment: 1m4f handicap win here latest, up 6lb, drop in trip and quicker ground queries now

runners.name: Iamimmaculate
runners.spotlight: Off the mark in Naas claiming maiden last September (1m, yld-sft); solid effort a month later on next start and handicap debut at Leopardstown (1m, yld-sft); up 4lb for that 0.5l defeat; better ground and longer trip are queries here and lacks a run, but top rider booked so check market.
runners.comment: Up 4lb for fine run on h'cap bow last Oct', off since, trip/ground queries, check market

races.race_name: Sean Cleary Memorial Handicap

runners.name: Unterberg
runners.spotlight: Six-time Dundalk winner, including three in a row there during February/March; turf record is modest (1-18), and he is 11lb higher than when fourth at Naas on his final start last season.
runners.comment: Six-time Dundalk winner, three wins in a row there February/March, turf record is modest

runners.name: Follow Me
runners.spotlight: Regressive since back-to-back wins at the Curragh early in the 2024 season; no impact in three runs last year; mid-field on reappearance at Limerick; well beaten over C&D last time; first time with cheekpieces
runners.comment: Regressive since back-to-back wins at the Curragh early in the 2024 season

runners.name: Flying Fortress
runners.spotlight: Enjoyed soft ground last year, runner-up over 1m at Galway, went one better on heavy at Listowel; below best this season; first-time blinkers failed to do the trick at Gowran.
runners.comment: Heavy-ground winner, below best this season, blinkers failed to do the trick at Gowran

runners.name: Liam Swagger
runners.spotlight: Useful juvenile hurdler for James Owen in 2024-25 season; won twice, and stayed on well for third (best of the home team) in the Fred Winter; AW winner, 0-6 on turf, placed four times; makes Irish debut after a long layoff and probably needs a bit further than this.
runners.comment: Useful Flat performer/smart juvenile hurdler in Britain, Irish debut after a long layoff

runners.name: Sinbad My Dad
runners.spotlight: Just one win from 29 attempts; consistent when making the frame on three occasions at Dundalk last November/December; failed to reproduce that form in two races at the same venue in January.
runners.comment: Just one win from 29 attempts, lost form on AW early in the year after three placed runs
runners.owner: Mrs Helen Butler & John A Griffin & Mrs Bernadette Fahey

runners.name: Grey Leader
runners.spotlight: Five-time winner for Joe Murphy; yet to find his form for this stable; reduced rating now (4lb below his last winning mark) but needs to show some spark before becoming a betting proposition again.
runners.comment: Five-time winner for Joe Murphy, yet to find his form for this stable, reduced mark now

runners.name: Vlhova
runners.spotlight: Consistent form last year; won at Killarney's July fixture and ended the campaign with seconds at Leopardstown and Dundalk; could make her presence felt if reappearing in peak shape
runners.comment: Consistent form last year, could make her presence felt if reappearing in peak shape

runners.name: Sir Jeremy
runners.spotlight: Improved from seasonal/yard debut when scoring over C&D last month; well beaten at Gowran since.
runners.comment: Improved from seasonal/yard debut when scoring over C&D last month, well beaten at Gowran

runners.name: Sonoran
runners.spotlight: Won 7f Tipperary maiden on debut last year; no further success, but holds a fair chance judged on his third at Leopardstown on his penultimate start; was drawn wide when unplaced at the same venue last time.
runners.comment: No further success since his debut at three, fair chance judged on his penultimate start

runners.name: Thatwilldoso
runners.spotlight: Won three handicaps last season (one of them over C&D) and went up aggregate 18lb in the process; yet to hit form in three runs this term; now back on her last winning mark; worth considering.
runners.comment: Won three handicaps last season, yet to hit form in three runs this term, reduced mark now

runners.name: Amplitude
runners.spotlight: Too high in the ratings since a Gowran maiden win last autumn; however, he ran well over 1m4f here on latest, form boosted when runner-up Obscenity continued a good spell of form with a Gowran success.
runners.comment: Too high in the ratings since a Gowran win last autumn, ran well over 1m4f here on latest

runners.name: Marta's Prince
runners.spotlight: Won twice in France; probably best watched since Nicola Burns prefers her father's other runner, the race-fit Grey Leader.
runners.comment: Won twice in France, probably best watched since Nicola Burns prefers Grey Leader

runners.name: Blackjack Hills
runners.spotlight: Showed significant improvement in first-time blinkers to win a handicap over an extended 1m at Gowran last October; proved he could handle a sound surface with a follow-up success at Dundalk in November; went up 14lb in total; further improvement feasible this term, but blinkers left off here.
runners.comment: Went up 14lb in total for two wins late last year, further improvement feasible this term

races.race_name: Watch Racing TV Irish EBF Lenebane Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.name: Shaool
runners.spotlight: Listed winner at Leopardstown (1m2f, heavy) last season; nowhere near that level in two runs this term, slowly away on both occasions; has form on good ground and at this trip so while she has a bit to find on the figures, she's not out of this if rediscovering her best.
runners.comment: Listed winner last year, below best this term, trip/ground fine, chance if back to best

runners.name: Starford
runners.spotlight: 4-10 on turf including  a handicap win here off 74 last July (10.5f, yielding); won the Group 3 Alleged Stakes at the Curragh in April (1m2f, soft); not a bad run in 10.5f Chester Group 2 latest (10.5f, good), shaping as though ready to step back up to this sort of trip; leading claims.
runners.comment: 4-10, course winner, landed Gr 3 on penultimate, good Gr'2 run latest, leading claims

runners.name: Chally Chute
runners.spotlight: Won this race (yielding) on first start for this stable last season and has held form well since; runner up in Irish Cesarewitch at the Curragh (2m1f, yielding) then landed the Group 3 Loughbrown Stakes there in November; close second in Limerick Listed race (12.5f, soft) on seasonal return 11 days ago; top rated and obvious chance, but could do with some rain.
runners.comment: Last year's winner, added Group 3 in November, fine Listed run latest, could do with rain

runners.name: Bosphorus Rose
runners.spotlight: Won three handicaps in Britain for Sir Mark Prescott; very pleasing start for this stable when a 2.75l third of 9 in a Cork Group 3 (1m4f, good) behind the promising Sparan Nua; a bit to find on paper here, but will love the ground and more black type could be on the cards.
runners.comment: Three h'cap wins in Britain, fine third in Cork Gr'3 on debut for this yard, can go well

runners.name: Abbey Actress
runners.spotlight: Off the mark for this yard in 1m1f Leopardstown handicap (soft) 19 days ago; all three of her wins have come in handicaps on soft/heavy and she was well held on her only previous try at this level at Gowran in May; cheekpieces go on; Shaool looks yard's best hope.
runners.comment: Third handicap win on soft/heavy at Leopardstown latest, ground a query, cheekpieces on

runners.name: Dreamkeeper
runners.spotlight: Built on promise shown in first two maiden starts to run away with a 1m4f Fairyhouse maiden (good) 18 days ago, her first run at this trip; this is much tougher, but she's totally unexposed at this distance and it wouldn't be a surprise were she to run well.
runners.comment: Dominant Fairyhouse maiden win latest, moves up in class now, unexposed, could go well
runners.owner: Mrs H Shanahan & Mrs Jane Shanahan

runners.name: Kalixa
runners.spotlight: Naas maiden (10.5f, yielding) winner for Dermot Weld last season; well held on sole previous try at Listed level and with an official rating of 82, this looks too ambitious on her seasonal return and stable debut.
runners.comment: Naas maiden winner, new yard, rated 82, lacks a run, this looks too ambitious

runners.name: Counting Coup
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten on debut in a Sligo bumper (2m2f, good) in May but left that behind to score on Flat bow in a Down Royal maiden (1m5f, soft) 10 days ago when 25-1, taking the scalp of Daddy Long Legs; stiffer task here, however, and quicker ground to deal with too.
runners.comment: Won on Flat debut at Down Royal (1m5f, soft), this tougher, quicker ground a query

races.race_name: Ladies Day At Roscommon On Monday, 6th July, 2026 Maiden

runners.name: Cracking Cloud
runners.spotlight: Rated 74 after three maiden runs at 9.5f on soft, yielding and good to yielding ground; hasn't shaped like he's been crying out for this longer trip the last twice and might prefer easier ground.
runners.comment: Rated 74, weakened over 9.5f the last twice, stamina to prove for this trip

runners.name: Ashikita
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten on debut at Dundalk in March 2024 for Dermot Weld; back from mammoth layoff with much improved effort on first start for this yard at the Curragh (1m4f, yielding) 27 days ago; should be sharper for that and can go closer now, once she doesn't 'bounce'.
runners.comment: Back from mammoth layoff with decent run at the Curragh, can go closer now

runners.name: Black Heather
runners.spotlight: Bumper and maiden hurdle winner for Harry Kelly last year; close third on sole Flat start at Bellewstown (1m4f, good) in April 2025; pulled up over timber when last seen last July; worth a market check on return.
runners.comment: Bumper and m'den hurdle winner, promise sole flat start, off since last July, check market

runners.name: Taj Crown
runners.spotlight: No show on debut at Dundalk last November; not beaten far on turf debut at Killarney (1m, good), staying on well; runner up when stepped up to 1m4f in Limerick claimer; soundly beaten in maiden there latest, but soft ground a possible reason for that; not discounted returned to a sounder surface.
runners.comment: Promise in first two runs this season, soft may not have suited latest, not discounted

runners.name: Seti One
runners.spotlight: Best run for Willie McCreery came in a 1m2f Curragh maiden (yld-sft) last September; tailed off on AW when last seen in December; new yard now; likely best watched unless the market speaks.
runners.comment: Promise in Curragh maiden for former yard, tailed off on AW when last seen, watch

runners.name: Steel Cut
runners.spotlight: Standout effort on penultimate at Dundalk (10.5f) when a 0.5l second of 12 (RPR 85); looked in need of further that day but disappointed upped to 1m5f at Down Royal latest, although soft ground may not have suited; blinkers go on now; chance.
runners.comment: Excellent run at Dundalk on penultimate, ground excuse latest, chance; blinkers

runners.name: Coolnagrattan
runners.spotlight: Definite promise on debut in a Punchestown bumper last month, weakening late on into an 8l fourth of 13 (RPR 97); could go well on first start in this sphere.
runners.comment: Promise on debut in Punchestown bumper, could go well on Flat bow

runners.name: Double Indian
runners.spotlight: 25-1 and tailed off in a Kilbeggan bumper on debut in April; can only be watched on Flat debut after that effort.
runners.comment: Tailed off when 25-1 on debut in Kilbeggan bumper in April, can only watch after that

runners.name: Lauro's Legend
runners.spotlight: Placed in one of six starts over timber and rated 103 in that sphere; not disgraced on Flat debut in 1m7f Leopardstown maiden (gd-fm) on latest; more needed down in trip.
runners.comment: 103 rated hurdler, okay effort on Flat debut latest, more needed back in trip

runners.name: Quint Major
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner and rated 105 over hurdles; not beaten far on Flat debut at Leopardstown in June 2025 (1m7f, good, RPR 80), but below that level when beaten 17.5l at Thurles in October; place shout if he repeats that Leopardstown effort.
runners.comment: Bumper winner, promise on Flat debut at Leopardstown, place shout if repeats that

runners.name: Elman
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose; very modest form so far in both codes; fell over hurdles at Limerick when last seen in March; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Modest form over jumps and on the Flat, fell over hurdles on latest, hard to fancy

runners.name: It's Good To Talk
runners.spotlight: Best run came on debut in a heavy ground Naas bumper; soundly beaten in Flat maidens the last twice including over C&D; qualifies for handicaps after this.
runners.comment: Best run in heavy ground bumper, well beaten in two Flat maidens, handicaps beckon

runners.name: Grennanstown Lady
runners.spotlight: Very modest so far over hurdles (rated 86) and in two previous runs on the Flat; seems safe to rule out.
runners.comment: Modest form over hurdles and in two runs on the Flat, seems safe to rule out

runners.name: Catherine Magennis
runners.spotlight: Shaped as though further would suit when a 16l seventh of 17 on debut at Gowran in April (9.5f, soft) and bred for this sort of trip too; related to good ground winners, so should handle conditions and eye-catching jockey booking; chance.
runners.comment: Promise on debut at Gowran (9.5f), bred for this longer trip, top rider booked, chance

runners.name: Sioux Grit
runners.spotlight: 0-18 on the Flat and rated just 32; would be a shock winner of a maiden.
runners.comment: 0-18 on the Flat, rated 32, would be a shock winner

runners.name: Boujee
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner rated 95 after five runs over hurdles; pulled up in that sphere on latest; beaten 35l on sole Flat start at Galway last July when 66-1; others make more appeal.
runners.comment: Bumper winner, rated 95 over hurdles, beaten 35l on sole Flat start, others preferred

races.race_name: ROA/RSA Shared Ownership Day Novices' Limited Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Captain Cool
runners.spotlight: Won first two starts over fences (2m1f, good) and shaped just as well when reverting to hurdles off 13lb lower, finishing runner-up at Southwell this month; latest second, over fences at Perth 23 days ago, was perfectly respectable but his current mark looks demanding.
runners.comment: Won first two chase starts and recent form is solid but current mark looks a tough one.

runners.name: Tellherthename
runners.spotlight: Useful hurdler for Ben Pauling, winning two of his ten starts; has quickly found his feet back over fences with back-to-back small-field wins, the latest with ease at Uttoxeter 17 days ago; up 6lb and may face a sterner test from Captain Cool but still the one to beat.
runners.comment: Back-to-back chase wins, latest at Uttoxeter; may face sterner test but the one to beat.

runners.name: Ice In The Veins
runners.spotlight: 2-7 over hurdles for Dan Skelton before being sold for £5,000 after bleeding from the nose and pulling up on chase debut at Bangor in May; made a satisfactory start for this stable when runner-up to Tellherthename at Uttoxeter two weeks ago but is 4lb out of the weights here.
runners.comment: Made an encouraging stable debut behind Tellherthename but needs more on these terms.

races.race_name: Racehorse Ownership From £25 At racingclub.com Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Square d'Alboni
runners.spotlight: 2yo Listed winner on the Flat; made a promising start over hurdles when third at Newbury (2m, good to soft) in January but was well beaten at short odds at Catterick (2m, soft) later that month; subsequently underwent wind surgery and, if able to reproduce his earlier effort, he's firmly in the mix on this comeback.
runners.comment: Promising Newbury third but well below that only start since; had wind surgery

runners.name: Mr Rafiki
runners.spotlight: He hinted at ability in two hurdle runs this spring following wind surgery, albeit beaten 25l and 15l, most recently when fourth of eight at Warwick (2m3f, good) in May; may be one for further down the line but has place claims.
runners.comment: Fourth at Warwick after wind surgery and has place claims, but needs more for the win

runners.name: Penzance
runners.spotlight: Has reached the frame in both hurdles starts but beaten 34l and 23l in the process; could be one for handicaps in due course but, in a race lacking depth, he may be capable of hitting the frame again.
runners.comment: Twice in frame over hurdles albeit well beaten; could hit frame again in a weak race

runners.name: Bound For Glory
runners.spotlight: Progressed nicely in three bumper runs, winning here (good to firm) on his latest start by a wide margin; appeals as the type to make an impact in this sphere and he's respected on hurdle debut, especially now stepping up in trip.
runners.comment: Progressive bumper winner; wide-margin latest success; respected on hurdle debut

runners.name: Usyk
runners.spotlight: Showed ability in early bumpers but form has gone the wrong way, including on both starts over hurdles this summer; best watched for now.
runners.comment: Showed early ability but struggling over hurdles; best watched for now

runners.name: Colibri Bleu
runners.spotlight: Beat one rival in a maiden point in March 2025 but failed to complete in five subsequent runs; showed some promise when 24l third on hurdle debut at Warwick (2m, good) this month but failed to build on that when well beaten over C&D two weeks ago.
runners.comment: Promising debut third but well beaten over C&D latest; has plenty to find

runners.name: Connemara Joe
runners.spotlight: Modest maiden pointer who was beaten 35l on hurdle debut at Warwick (2m, good) this month; has plenty to find and it is easy to look elsewhere.
runners.comment: Beaten 35l on Warwick hurdle debut and has lots to find; easy to look elsewhere

runners.name: King Troy
runners.spotlight: Has failed to complete in three starts in points and finished tailed off on sole hurdle start at Hereford (2m4f, good) in March last year; hard to recommend on stable debut.
runners.comment: Failed to complete in points and tailed off on hurdle debut; hard to fancy

races.race_name: SolidCam UK Partner Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Tyson
runners.spotlight: Two Class 5 wins to his name, most recently at Huntingdon (extended 2m4f, good to firm) in May, and his follow-up effort under a penalty was a respectable first try at 3m2f; drop back in trip should suit and although his revised mark has now taken effect he is still respected.
runners.comment: Two wins; return to shorter trip can suit; revised mark makes this tougher but respected

runners.name: Jackstell
runners.spotlight: Won three consecutive small-field races in the autumn, the latest a Plumpton handicap (2m4f, good), but has been well below par on his two subsequent starts, including at Market Rasen in May after a break; remains feasibly treated if the cheekpieces and tongue-tie prove a positive addition.
runners.comment: Won three small-field races in the autumn but well below par since; headgear could help.

runners.name: Snatch A Glance
runners.spotlight: Yet to add to his 2m Ludlow maiden hurdle win but has run with credit since, most recently on handicap debut at Market Rasen when staying on over 2m4f after racing wide; likely to be involved off an unchanged mark if stamina holds up over this longer trip.
runners.comment: 2m Ludlow hurdle winner; creditable runs since and may be involved if staying new trip.

runners.name: Prince De Juilley
runners.spotlight: Returned to form in the autumn, landing a deserved C&D success in October and running well in defeat on two subsequent starts up to around 3m; remains on a tough mark but should be all the sharper for this month's reappearance.
runners.comment: Returned to form last autumn with C&D win; should be sharper for recent reappearance.

runners.name: Lion Of The Desert
runners.spotlight: Recorded back-to-back wins at Ffos Las (2m4f, good) in the spring and has continued in good form off higher marks since, most recently going down by a head over C&D last month; nudged up another 3lb but remains hard to ignore in current form.
runners.comment: Back-to-back Ffos Las wins in the spring; head second over C&D latest, up another 3lb.

runners.name: Balboa
runners.spotlight: Lacks consistency but capitalised on a reduced mark when ending a near three-year drought at Ayr (extended 2m4f, soft) in April, but has failed to reproduce that effort since and the 6lb rise is not the chief concern; however, he could still go well if at his best.
runners.comment: Ended long drought at Ayr in April but below that since; could still go well if at best.

runners.name: The Gypsy Davey
runners.spotlight: Won two chases at Hereford (2m5f, good) in April and May 2025 but has been more miss than hit since, with both runs this year underwhelming; reverts to hurdles with a visor added off a fair mark but needs to turn things around.
runners.comment: Back over hurdles off a fair mark but visor needs to spark a revival.

runners.name: Minella Double
runners.spotlight: 2-11 over hurdles and produced his best effort in a while when runner-up at Huntingdon (extended 2m4f, good) in May last year, but was pulled up on his return from a break in February, which tempers enthusiasm; returns from another layoff with a bit too much to prove at present.
runners.comment: 2-11 over hurdles; runner-up in May last year but pulled up since, others preferred.

races.race_name: Davisons Law Moving You Forward Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Newport
runners.spotlight: Comfortably doubled his hurdle score when winning at Hexham (2m, good) nine days ago, escapes a panalty for that success and Tom Midgley can claim his 3lb allowance here; another bold bid looks likely.
runners.comment: Hexham winner nine days ago; escapes penalty and looks set for another bold bid.

runners.name: Katzoff
runners.spotlight: 46-race maiden under all codes, including 19 starts over hurdles; runner-up at Clonmel (2m1f, yielding) in April but failed to build on that next time; makes stable debut for a leading yard and, though he has plenty to prove, it'll be interesting to see what they can do with him.
runners.comment: Maiden after 46 starts, but makes debut for top stable which makes him of interest.

runners.name: Stellarmasterpiece
runners.spotlight: Five-time Flat winner (1m4f); justified strong market support when opening hurdles account here (2m, good to firm) in May, under Sean Bowen and shaping as though this longer trip would suit; up 6lb but remains capable of better after just eight starts in this sphere; leading contender.
runners.comment: Opened hurdles account over 2m here in May; 6lb higher but capable of better; key player.

runners.name: Yanka Blue
runners.spotlight: 11-race maiden, though placed on three occasions; has offered little encouragement in three runs since joining this stable, including when tried in cheekpieces last time.
runners.comment: 11-race maiden who has offered little encouragement in three runs for current yard.

runners.name: Winston's Oath
runners.spotlight: Best effort over hurdles when third of six at Fakenham (2m, good) in April but has failed to build on that and has been well held on both handicap starts; others have more obvious claims.
runners.comment: Best hurdle effort when third at Fakenham but well held in handicaps since.

runners.name: Charlie My Boy
runners.spotlight: 3-19 over hurdles, the latest coming at Huntingdon last May off 5lb higher; no match for Tyson (down to go in the 7.18) in his repeat bid in May on return from a break, though that was still encouraging; on a workable mark if able to build on that effort.
runners.comment: 3-19 over hurdles; encouraging return last month and not out of this if building on it.

runners.name: Zhang Fei
runners.spotlight: Struggled after returning to this stable last season but has shown more encouraging signs this spring, running respectably on the Flat before a better effort in a Cartmel selling hurdle (2m1f, good) in May; faces a tougher task back in open handicap company under top weight but has place claims.
runners.comment: Better on Flat and over hurdles recently but top weight in stronger company asks for more.

runners.name: Brooklyn Lullaby
runners.spotlight: Showed little in three hurdle runs last year but returned from a break with a more encouraging effort when fifth on handicap debut at Southwell (2m, good) recently; needs another step forward but has place claims.
runners.comment: More encouraging on handicap debut; another step forward could see her involved.

runners.name: Western Soldier
runners.spotlight: Won at Wincanton (1m7f, soft) off this mark in November but failed to build on it afterwards; has offered more in two runs since returning from a short break but needs another step forward to figure.
runners.comment: Won off this mark in November; better lately but needs another step forward.

runners.name: Get The Value
runners.spotlight: Completed back-to-back wins last spring, including over 2m here, but has been badly out of form since returning from a break in December and failed to justify market support when pulled up at Newton Abbot this month; on a good mark but hard to recommend in current form.
runners.comment: Back-to-back wins last spring but badly out of form since; hard to recommend.

races.race_name: Laura Horsfall Racing Club Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Delpotro
runners.spotlight: Front-runner whose two wins last year came at Sedgefield when making all; has had excuses since returning from a short break in May, possibly stretching his stamina over 2m5f first time and finding the race coming too soon latest; only 1lb higher than for his latest win but needs to step up.
runners.comment: Front-runner with two wins at Sedgefield in 2025 but below best since back from break.

runners.name: Lady Kara
runners.spotlight: Defied a double penalty when completing her hat-trick over hurdles at Newton Abbot (2m1f, good) 18 days ago; connections wasting little time in switching to fences for handicap debut and she looks to have been crying out for this longer trip; on a workable mark and could improve again.
runners.comment: Makes handicap/chase debut off a workable mark and will be well suited by the longer trip.

runners.name: Lakefield Flyer
runners.spotlight: Dual chase winner but has yet to match that level in three starts this year, including when seemingly not staying 3m at Hexham 24 days ago; drop back in trip should suit but he needs to stage a revival.
runners.comment: Dual chase winner but below best this year; drop in trip may help but a revival is needed.

runners.name: Glenmalure Flyer
runners.spotlight: Maiden point winner at the 11th attempt prior to winning stable debut at Bangor (3m, good; refitted tongue-tie) four days ago; carries a penalty and drops back in trip but looks a big danger.
runners.comment: Won at Bangor four days ago on stable debut; carries a penalty but looks a big danger

runners.name: Therhythmofthenite
runners.spotlight: Promising fourth on hurdle debut but well held in two subsequent starts for Martin Brassil and little impact when switched to fences on handicap/stable debut at Warwick (2m4f, good) after a break; still early days but he doesn't look particularly well treated on that evidence.
runners.comment: Fourth on hurdle debut but well beaten since, including on recent chase/handicap debut.

races.race_name: Sunday 12th July Is Family Day Mares' National Hunt Flat Race (Cond'/Amateurs) (Cat 2 Elim') (GBB)

runners.name: Redbarn
runners.spotlight: Beaten favourite in a couple of Irish points but snapped up for £25,000 in May and made no mistake when winning bumper debut at Worcester (good) three weeks ago, scoring with a bit in hand; penalised for that success but, with Harry Atkins' claim offsetting the bulk of that, he's the one to beat.
runners.comment: Won Worcester bumper on rules/stable debut with a bit in hand; penalised but one to beat.

runners.name: Cool Million
runners.spotlight: Sixth foal; half-sister to 2m5f chase winner Another Million; promising fifth of ten on last month's Southwell bumper debut (2m, good), shaping as though the experience would do her good; open to improvement and could make the frame.
runners.comment: Promising fifth at Southwell on debut; should improve and could make the frame.

runners.name: Ocean Rose
runners.spotlight: Well beaten on this month's Newton Abbot debut (2m1f, good to soft) and has plenty to find on that form; pedigree suggests she'll be seen to better effect over further in due course.
runners.comment: Struggled on debut and will likely to be seen to better effect over longer trips in time.

runners.name: Upton Fern
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; closely related to useful 2m7f hurdle winner Doc Penfro and a half-sister to 2m5f-3m2f hurdle/chase winner Juniper; dam won bumper and over hurdles/fences; appeals on pedigree but is likely to need a stiffer test of stamina to be seen at her best.
runners.comment: Well-related newcomer but may need a stiffer test of stamina to show best.

runners.name: Frankie's Flame
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam 7.5f winner (RPR 80), half-sister to useful winners Regulation (1m Flat and 2m-2m4f hurdle/chase) and Decorative (6f-1m); makes some appeal on pedigree and market support would heighten interest.
runners.comment: Makes some appeal on pedigree and market support on debut would heighten interest.

runners.name: Thetype Istitle
runners.spotlight: Dam unraced half-sister to high-class bumper and jumps performer Outlander and smart hurdle/chase winner Western Leader; bought for 13,000euros after a runner-up finish on her third Irish point start and that experience should stand her in good stead on debut under rules; respected.
runners.comment: Irish point runner-up, related to smart sorts; respected on debut for David Pipe.

runners.name: Queens Glance
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to the yard's fair hurdler I'm A Starman; faced an impossible task leaving the back straight on her Worcester debut (good; tongue-tie) 19 days ago but kept on with some purpose to finish a never-dangerous fourth; likely to come on for that experience and merits respect.
runners.comment: Shaped with definite promise when fourth on Worcester debut; should improve for the run.

races.race_name: Town And Country Cakes Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C And D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Egyptian Pharaoh
runners.spotlight: Twice placed over shorter at Dundalk in the spring before changing hands and gelded; edged left, away from the rail, at Windsor for his new yard a fortnight ago (first-time tongue-tie; made most; Adalo well behind); anti-clockwise course should help.
runners.comment: Solid debut for new yard recently (first-time tongue-tie; made most; Adalo well behind)

runners.name: Mother Dear
runners.spotlight: Needed her debut experience, when a big price over 7f (warm Newbury maiden), and took a good step forward upped to this trip at Goodwood last month; should have more to offer.
runners.comment: Good step forward upped to this trip at Goodwood last month; should have more to offer

runners.name: Adalo
runners.spotlight: Well held on his debut (1m) early last month and ran below market expectations upped to this trip a fortnight ago (behind Egyptian Pharaoh); trainer has an exceptional record here (16-35) but that's the main positive.
runners.comment: Two tame runs; trainer has an exceptional record here (16-35) but that's the main positive

runners.name: Celestial Cen
runners.spotlight: Like Liberate shaped nicely coming from off a steady pace on her Lingfield (1m2f, soft) debut early in the month; started a big price that day and is entitled to improve.
runners.comment: Shaped nicely on her Lingfield (1m2f, soft) debut early in the month;  entitled to improve

runners.name: Dialstone
runners.spotlight: Gelded brother to the yard's decent bumper (on debut)/1m4f-1m6f winner Gnomon/half-brother to their 7f-1m winner Newbolt; trainer does well here (16-57 with 3yos) and the market will guide.
runners.comment: Gelded brother to yard's decent bumper (on debut)/1m4f-1m6f winner Gnomon; market useful
runners.owner: A D G Oldrey & G C Hartigan

runners.name: Just Johnny
runners.spotlight: Looked to have stamina issues in bumpers; been gelded since last seen a year ago and is likely one for handicaps further down the line (rare Flat runner for his yard).
runners.comment: Gelded since last seen a year ago and is likely one for handicaps further down the line

runners.name: Liberate
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to two French winners who shaped fairly behind Celestial Cen on his soft-ground debut over this trip early in the month, having got behind after a slow start; will have learned from that.
runners.comment: Shaped fairly behind Celestial Cen early in the month having got behind after a slow start

runners.name: Tinkety Tonk
runners.spotlight: Remote last on his debut over this trip and again well held upped to 1m6f last time; likely he'll find life easier once handicapping for a yard also responsible for leading fancy Mother Dear.
runners.comment: Likely he'll find life easier once handicapping for yard also responsible for Mother Dear

races.race_name: West Wales Properties Pembrokeshire Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Dark Whisper
runners.spotlight: Pleasing debut when a clear second (Impierious third) to a useful prospect at Newmarket in April; bit below that level when some way off his two market rivals next time but is one of the likelier winners for a yard going well.
runners.comment: Decent debut; below that level next time but one of the likelier winners for in-form yard

runners.name: Impierious
runners.spotlight: Well-related gelding who finished a few lengths behind Dark Whisper when both made their debut at Newmarket in the spring; ran to a similar level in a small field at Doncaster early in the month and has fair claims on either effort.
runners.comment: Behind Dark Whisper on debut; ran to a similar level latest; fair claims on either effort
runners.owner: Hasmonean Racing, S Hope & S Barrow

runners.name: Kino Plasmat
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to three-time Group 1 winner (Hong Kong) Waikuku and the yard's 1m2f Royal Ascot winner (Group 3) Waipiro; easy to back and looked to need her debut experience a few weeks ago (7f, good to soft), when shaping nicely; high on the shortlist.
runners.comment: Needed her debut experience a few weeks ago (7f), when shaping nicely; shortlisted

runners.name: Lean d'Aislingean
runners.spotlight: Did too much too soon on his debut and bettered that at Windsor last time, when going at it up front for a long way with the long odds-on favourite; that effort sees him enter the equation for a yard among the winners.
runners.comment: Better at Windsor; that effort sees him enter the equation for a yard among the winners

runners.name: Vincenzo Bellini
runners.spotlight: Gelded, early-foaled 28,000gns 2yo who's the first foal of Shale, a Group 1 juvenile winner in Ireland (7f); stable (also saddle Elenaya) form might be better and he'd need to be above average to take this on debut.
runners.comment: First foal of Shale, a Group 1 2yo winner in Ireland (7f); would need to be above average

runners.name: Next Guilian
runners.spotlight: Some way off the front pair when third in a German maiden last November (1m1f, soft); 40-1, ran as the market suggested he might on his first start since being gelded a few weeks ago; handicaps are likely next on the agenda.
runners.comment: Well beaten on his comeback a few weeks ago; handicaps are likely next on the agenda

runners.name: Ingemar
runners.spotlight: Beaten a long way on his debut early in the month but pulled hard over 1m2f and didn't see it out; should fare better but will need a good step forward if he's to get involved.
runners.comment: Should fare better but will need a good step forward if he's to get involved

runners.name: Elenaya
runners.spotlight: Tailed-off last in two runs, spread well apart, for the Newland/Insole yard last year; goes in a first-time tongue-tie back from six months off and likely she'll find life easier once handicapping.
runners.comment: Tailed-off last in two runs, spread well apart, for the Newland/Insole yard last year

races.race_name: West Wales Properties Mayfair Classified Stakes

runners.name: King Of The Dance
runners.spotlight: Infrequent winner who was again turned over as favourite when never happy behind Prince Ali last time; wants a good test over this trip these days and others make greater appeal.
runners.comment: Never happy behind Prince Ali last time; wants a good test over this trip these days

runners.name: Prince Ali
runners.spotlight: Six-time AW winner who sprung a surprise under Zoe Lewis when having a rare turf start (1m, soft) early in the month (back in cheekpieces); this trip's a bare minimum and history suggests he's no good thing to back up that effort.
runners.comment: Sprung a surprise under Zoe Lewis last time; this trip's a bare minimum now

runners.name: Spirited Dancer
runners.spotlight: Back in form since dropped to this level and proved 7f within range at Brighton last week, despite not looking in love with the course; her claims are obvious under Billy Loughnane (8-41 for the yard) as long as there's not too much rain.
runners.comment: Back in form since dropped to this level; her claims are obvious under Billy Loughnane

runners.name: Blue Point Express
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent filly who offered more on her second run back a fortnight ago, when finding 6f an inadequate test (sole win came over this trip on soft ground); bit to find at the weights but is capable if on a going day.
runners.comment: Inconsistent filly; bit to find at the weights but is capable if on a going day

runners.name: A Rose Adaay
runners.spotlight: Well drawn when third at Chepstow early in the month, wasn't at the same course last time; won't mind the drop from 1m and is one to consider for 5lb claimer Harry Vigors, who's enjoying a purple patch.
runners.comment: Won't mind drop from 1m; one to consider for Harry Vigors, who's enjoying a purple patch

runners.name: Belltony
runners.spotlight: Not beaten far on his turf/handicap debut a fortnight ago but didn't achieve much at a big price (no real improvement on his AW work).
runners.comment: Not beaten far on handicap debut a fortnight ago but didn't achieve much at a big price

runners.name: Mokata
runners.spotlight: Well supported down to this level at Brighton early in the month, when unlucky not to go closer; conversely, proved easy to back at the same course last time, when slowly away and soon behind; much will hinge on how she takes to first-time headgear.
runners.comment: Disappointing last time; much will hinge on how she takes to first-time headgear

runners.name: Snow Day
runners.spotlight: Huge prices for four runs, finishing well back each time, three times after fluffing the start; little to recommend her.
runners.comment: Huge prices for four runs, finishing well back each time, three times after a slow start

runners.name: Angel's Call
runners.spotlight: Regressed sharply last year and has been tried over various trips of late; one of his better efforts over 1m here last Tuesday but is worse off back out of handicap company and no certainty to repeat the dose.
runners.comment: Regressed sharply last year and is worse off back out of handicap company

races.race_name: WWP 34 Years Finding Customers Their Dream Homes Handicap

runners.name: Cypriot Diaspora
runners.spotlight: Two wins and a second to her name in three runs here, all over C&D; slid to a dangerous mark now, being 8lb lower than when last successful, but she's lost her way this year; soft ground ideal, so the more rain the better for her.
runners.comment: Two wins and a second to her name in three runs here; well treated; soft ground ideal

runners.name: Quilt
runners.spotlight: Well-beaten third on her turf/handicap debut last month before not seeing out 9.5f on Tapeta last time; this is a bit easier back over 7f but she's starting to look exposed.
runners.comment: Didn't see out 9.5f on Tapeta last time; this is a bit easier but starting to look exposed

runners.name: Kaleidoscope Eyes
runners.spotlight: Surprise winner of a heavy-ground 0-55 handicap over C&D last autumn; didn't offer a lot on her comeback early in the month and is hard to recommend after that.
runners.comment: Didn't offer a lot on her comeback early in the month and is hard to recommend after that

runners.name: Tonal
runners.spotlight: Won five times during the AW season; much better treated on turf now but has never been as good or consistent on grass, and he ran poorly at Yarmouth when last seen; Billy Loughnane is a positive booking but he wouldn't want too much rain.
runners.comment: Never been as good or consistent on grass; hasn't run at shy of 1m in a long time

runners.name: Medyg
runners.spotlight: Fair in three runs on Tapeta last backend (6f-7f) before being gelded; inclined to overrace on his turf/handicap debut (6f) back from a break early last month and still has a bit of potential for a yard that was back among the winners on Sunday.
runners.comment: Still has a bit of potential for a yard that was back among the winners on Sunday

runners.name: Dappled Light
runners.spotlight: Three-time Bath winner who has a win and a second to his name in two runs here (both 1m); adaptable regarding tactics but hasn't run over 7f since his 3yo days in Ireland and he's drawn widest back up to a career-high mark; wouldn't want much rain.
runners.comment: Not run over 7f since his 3yo days and he's drawn widest back up to a career-high mark

runners.name: Arnaz
runners.spotlight: Lost his way for this yard, pulling hard the past couple of times; conditions fine off a career-low mark but he has something to prove back from a short break, during which time he had a wind operation.
runners.comment: Conditions fine off a career-low mark but something to prove back from a wind operation

runners.name: Red Moon
runners.spotlight: Possible that soft ground didn't suit when beaten at a short price over C&D a fortnight ago; also possible it came too soon after his small-field Salisbury win but that race isn't working out; bit to prove currently.
runners.comment: Beaten at short price over C&D (soft) a fortnight ago but that may have come too soon

runners.name: Sofian
runners.spotlight: Best efforts come over C&D, including a surprise win off 2lb lower last August; absent for three months since an ordinary AW comeback but plenty of rain would suit and it wouldn't be a shock were he to pop up.
runners.comment: Plenty of rain would suit and it wouldn't be a shock were he to pop up

races.race_name: West Wales Properties Carmarthenshire Handicap

runners.name: Tanaka
runners.spotlight: No penalty for winning a weak apprentice handicap at Beverley last Tuesday but she scrambled home at a big price there and is unlikely to go up much anyway; now has to back it up and doubt she'd want rain (taken out on good to soft ground recently).
runners.comment: No penalty for winning a weak apprentice handicap last Tuesday; now has to back that up

runners.name: Forever Glamorous
runners.spotlight: Expensive to follow since handicapping but valid excuses as favourite last time, when finding the leaders not coming back from a wide stall; been found a weak 0-55 handicap in first-time headgear.
runners.comment: Expensive to follow since handicapping; found a weak 0-55 handicap in first-time headgear

runners.name: My Mate Mackley
runners.spotlight: Poor in three AW starts before gelded; much better when dead-heating in a weak, small-field Chepstow handicap last month but failed to build on that on very soft ground last time; goes in first-time headgear under Billy Loughnane (first ride for the yard).
runners.comment: Beaten favourite on very soft ground last time; goes in first-time headgear tonight

runners.name: Magnificent Mel
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent maiden who's been turned over at short prices a few times of late, the past twice in 0-50 classified company; a poor effort 12 days ago will need forgiving for a yard also responsible for Follow My Heart.
runners.comment: Inconsistent maiden; a poor effort 12 days ago will need forgiving

runners.name: Follow My Heart
runners.spotlight: Found more behind a better-fancied stablemate on her handicap debut but didn't build on that last time; has a first crack at 5f under Cieren Fallon, who's currently 0-7 for the yard.
runners.comment: Yet to achieve much; first crack at 5f under Cieren Fallon (currently 0-7 for the yard)

runners.name: Kodi K
runners.spotlight: First signs of life on his handicap debut 12 days ago, when dropped from 6f; that was a weak, small-field affair but he's been dropped 2lb and it gives him something to build on.
runners.comment: First sign of life on handicap debut 12 days ago; that gives him something to build on

runners.name: Runamara
runners.spotlight: Sharply regressive since winning a soft-ground Bath maiden (5.7f) last autumn, turning in three tame efforts since returned to turf; this is easier again but it's hard to find many positives.
runners.comment: Three tame runs since returned to turf; this easier again but hard to find many positives

races.race_name: West Wales Properties Ceredigion Handicap

runners.name: Simiyann
runners.spotlight: C&D win last August came off an 8lb higher mark; the booking of Billy Loughnane will draw some in, back to the visor worn for his two AW victories, but two poor efforts on turf lately will need overlooking.
runners.comment: C&D win last August came off 8lb higher; two poor runs on turf lately need overlooking

runners.name: Sapphire Sirocco
runners.spotlight: In good form for her new yard back on turf, appreciating the stiffer test when winning off 2lb lower last time; having vastly experienced 5lb claimer Ryan Kavanagh up won't hurt and she's shortlisted.
runners.comment: In good form for her new yard back on turf; 2lb rise is fair and she's shortlisted

runners.name: Merrijig
runners.spotlight: Done a lot of his winning later in the year but offered more in the returning blinkers last time, back from a couple of months off; boasts a decent record when dipping below 0-70 company and won't mind any rain.
runners.comment: Boasts a decent record when dipping below 0-70 company and won't mind any rain

runners.name: Chillhi
runners.spotlight: Been around a bit and has regressed sharply for this yard, mainly over hurdles; Sapphire Sirocco looks the more likkely of the stable pair.
runners.comment: Been around a bit and has regressed sharply for this yard, mainly over hurdles

runners.name: Cogital
runners.spotlight: Veteran who's done a lot of his winning in the summer and always liked it round here; back in form over hurdles last time and is 2lb lower than when going in here last July, so he needs a second look.
runners.comment: Back in form over hurdles last time and is 2lb lower than when going in here last July

runners.name: Adrian
runners.spotlight: Nothing like as good or consistent these days but back in form last time (1m4f, 0-50) to confirm his liking for this course; gives his trainer, who saddles two others, a decent hand but wouldn't want the ground too soft.
runners.comment: Back in form last time (1m4f, 0-50) to confirm his liking for this course; player

runners.name: Bulldog Spirit
runners.spotlight: Dual winner last year, latterly off 4lb higher, who's running in a 0-65 for the first time; finished last on his latest start but wasn't beaten far and Cieren Fallon (0-5 for the yard) is a positive booking; ought to fare better.
runners.comment: Dual winner last year, latterly off 4lb higher; ought to fare better under Cieren Fallon

runners.name: Oceanides
runners.spotlight: String of poor efforts to date, latterly when a remote last behind Adrian in a 0-50 here; the new trip will need to bring about a lot of improvement.
runners.comment: Poor to date; new trip will need to bring about a lot of improvement

runners.name: Golden Flame
runners.spotlight: Missed a chunk of his career and has been steadily regressive since; hit and miss last backend and is entitled to need this at his age after nine months off; wouldn't want too much rain.
runners.comment: Hit and miss last backend and is entitled to need this at his age after nine months off

runners.name: Oakley
runners.spotlight: Been in good form over hurdles; having a rare start on the Flat and looks fairly treated, so he needs considering.
runners.comment: In good form over hurdles; looks fairly treated back on the Flat, so he needs considering
